37
38/* These parameters are set by GUC */
39int			from_collapse_limit;
40int			join_collapse_limit;
41
42
43/*
44 * deconstruct_jointree requires multiple passes over the join tree, because we
45 * need to finish computing JoinDomains before we start distributing quals.
46 * As long as we have to do that, other information such as the relevant
47 * qualscopes might as well be computed in the first pass too.
48 *
49 * deconstruct_recurse recursively examines the join tree and builds a List
50 * (in depth-first traversal order) of JoinTreeItem structs, which are then
51 * processed iteratively by deconstruct_distribute.  If there are outer
52 * joins, non-degenerate outer join clauses are processed in a third pass
53 * deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals.
54 *
55 * The JoinTreeItem structs themselves can be freed at the end of
56 * deconstruct_jointree, but do not modify or free their substructure,
57 * as the relid sets may also be pointed to by RestrictInfo and
58 * SpecialJoinInfo nodes.
59 */
60typedef struct JoinTreeItem
61{
62	/* Fields filled during deconstruct_recurse: */
63	Node	   *jtnode;			/* jointree node to examine */
64	JoinDomain *jdomain;		/* join domain for its ON/WHERE clauses */
65	struct JoinTreeItem *jti_parent;	/* JoinTreeItem for this node's
66										 * parent, or NULL if it's the top */
67	Relids		qualscope;		/* base+OJ Relids syntactically included in
68								 * this jointree node */
69	Relids		inner_join_rels;	/* base+OJ Relids syntactically included
70									 * in inner joins appearing at or below
71									 * this jointree node */
72	Relids		left_rels;		/* if join node, Relids of the left side */
73	Relids		right_rels;		/* if join node, Relids of the right side */
74	Relids		nonnullable_rels;	/* if outer join, Relids of the
75									 * non-nullable side */
76	/* Fields filled during deconstruct_distribute: */
77	SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo;	/* if outer join, its SpecialJoinInfo */
78	List	   *oj_joinclauses; /* outer join quals not yet distributed */
79	List	   *lateral_clauses;	/* quals postponed from children due to
80									 * lateral references */
81} JoinTreeItem;
82
83/*
84 * Compatibility info for one GROUP BY item, precomputed for use by
85 * remove_useless_groupby_columns() when matching unique-index columns against
86 * GROUP BY items.
87 */
88typedef struct GroupByColInfo
89{
90	AttrNumber	attno;			/* var->varattno */
91	List	   *eq_opfamilies;	/* mergejoin opfamilies of sgc->eqop */
92	Oid			coll;			/* var->varcollid */
93} GroupByColInfo;
94
95
96static void extract_lateral_references(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *brel,
97									   Index rtindex);
98static List *deconstruct_recurse(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode,
99								 JoinDomain *parent_domain,
100								 JoinTreeItem *parent_jtitem,
101								 List **item_list);
102static void deconstruct_distribute(PlannerInfo *root, JoinTreeItem *jtitem);
103static void process_security_barrier_quals(PlannerInfo *root,
104										   int rti, JoinTreeItem *jtitem);
105static void mark_rels_nulled_by_join(PlannerInfo *root, Index ojrelid,
106									 Relids lower_rels);
107static SpecialJoinInfo *make_outerjoininfo(PlannerInfo *root,
108										   Relids left_rels, Relids right_rels,
109										   Relids inner_join_rels,
110										   JoinType jointype, Index ojrelid,
111										   List *clause);
112static void compute_semijoin_info(PlannerInfo *root, SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
113								  List *clause);
114static void deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals(PlannerInfo *root,
115											List *jtitems,
116											JoinTreeItem *jtitem);
117static void distribute_quals_to_rels(PlannerInfo *root, List *clauses,
118									 JoinTreeItem *jtitem,
119									 SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
120									 Index security_level,
121									 Relids qualscope,
122									 Relids ojscope,
123									 Relids outerjoin_nonnullable,
124									 Relids incompatible_relids,
125									 bool allow_equivalence,
126									 bool has_clone,
127									 bool is_clone,
128									 List **postponed_oj_qual_list);
129static void distribute_qual_to_rels(PlannerInfo *root, Node *clause,
130									JoinTreeItem *jtitem,
131									SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
132									Index security_level,
133									Relids qualscope,
134									Relids ojscope,
135									Relids outerjoin_nonnullable,
136									Relids incompatible_relids,
137									bool allow_equivalence,
138									bool has_clone,
139									bool is_clone,
140									List **postponed_oj_qual_list);
141static bool check_redundant_nullability_qual(PlannerInfo *root, Node *clause);
142static Relids get_join_domain_min_rels(PlannerInfo *root, Relids domain_relids);
143static void check_mergejoinable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo);
144static void check_hashjoinable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo);
145static void check_memoizable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo);
146
147
148/*****************************************************************************
149 *
150 *	 JOIN TREES
151 *
152 *****************************************************************************/
153
154/*
155 * add_base_rels_to_query
156 *
157 *	  Scan the query's jointree and create baserel RelOptInfos for all
158 *	  the base relations (e.g., table, subquery, and function RTEs)
159 *	  appearing in the jointree.
160 *
161 * The initial invocation must pass root->parse->jointree as the value of
162 * jtnode.  Internally, the function recurses through the jointree.
163 *
164 * At the end of this process, there should be one baserel RelOptInfo for
165 * every non-join RTE that is used in the query.  Some of the baserels
166 * may be appendrel parents, which will require additional "otherrel"
167 * RelOptInfos for their member rels, but those are added later.
168 */
169void
170add_base_rels_to_query(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode)
171{
172	if (jtnode == NULL)
173		return;
174	if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
175	{
176		int			varno = ((RangeTblRef *) jtnode)->rtindex;
177
178		(void) build_simple_rel(root, varno, NULL);
179	}
180	else if (IsA(jtnode, FromExpr))
181	{
182		FromExpr   *f = (FromExpr *) jtnode;
183		ListCell   *l;
184
185		foreach(l, f->fromlist)
186			add_base_rels_to_query(root, lfirst(l));
187	}
188	else if (IsA(jtnode, JoinExpr))
189	{
190		JoinExpr   *j = (JoinExpr *) jtnode;
191
192		add_base_rels_to_query(root, j->larg);
193		add_base_rels_to_query(root, j->rarg);
194	}
195	else
196		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
197			 (int) nodeTag(jtnode));
198}
199
200/*
201 * add_other_rels_to_query
202 *	  create "otherrel" RelOptInfos for the children of appendrel baserels
203 *
204 * At the end of this process, there should be RelOptInfos for all relations
205 * that will be scanned by the query.
206 */
207void
208add_other_rels_to_query(PlannerInfo *root)
209{
210	int			rti;
211
212	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
213	{
214		RelOptInfo *rel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
215		RangeTblEntry *rte = root->simple_rte_array[rti];
216
217		/* there may be empty slots corresponding to non-baserel RTEs */
218		if (rel == NULL)
219			continue;
220
221		/* Ignore any "otherrels" that were already added. */
222		if (rel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
223			continue;
224
225		/* If it's marked as inheritable, look for children. */
226		if (rte->inh)
227			expand_inherited_rtentry(root, rel, rte, rti);
228	}
229}
230
231
232/*****************************************************************************
233 *
234 *	 TARGET LISTS
235 *
236 *****************************************************************************/
237
238/*
239 * build_base_rel_tlists
240 *	  Add targetlist entries for each var needed in the query's final tlist
241 *	  (and HAVING clause, if any) to the appropriate base relations.
242 *
243 * We mark such vars as needed by "relation 0" to ensure that they will
244 * propagate up through all join plan steps.
245 */
246void
247build_base_rel_tlists(PlannerInfo *root, List *final_tlist)
248{
249	List	   *tlist_vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) final_tlist,
250											 PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
251											 PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
252											 PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
253
254	if (tlist_vars != NIL)
255	{
256		add_vars_to_targetlist(root, tlist_vars, bms_make_singleton(0));
257		list_free(tlist_vars);
258	}
259
260	/*
261	 * If there's a HAVING clause, we'll need the Vars it uses, too.  Note
262	 * that HAVING can contain Aggrefs but not WindowFuncs.
263	 */
264	if (root->parse->havingQual)
265	{
266		List	   *having_vars = pull_var_clause(root->parse->havingQual,
267												  PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
268												  PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
269
270		if (having_vars != NIL)
271		{
272			add_vars_to_targetlist(root, having_vars,
273								   bms_make_singleton(0));
274			list_free(having_vars);
275		}
276	}
277}
278
279/*
280 * add_vars_to_targetlist
281 *	  For each variable appearing in the list, add it to the owning
282 *	  relation's targetlist if not already present, and mark the variable
283 *	  as being needed for the indicated join (or for final output if
284 *	  where_needed includes "relation 0").
285 *
286 *	  The list may also contain PlaceHolderVars.  These don't necessarily
287 *	  have a single owning relation; we keep their attr_needed info in
288 *	  root->placeholder_list instead.  Find or create the associated
289 *	  PlaceHolderInfo entry, and update its ph_needed.
290 *
291 *	  See also add_vars_to_attr_needed.
292 */
293void
294add_vars_to_targetlist(PlannerInfo *root, List *vars,
295					   Relids where_needed)
296{
297	ListCell   *temp;
298
299	Assert(!bms_is_empty(where_needed));
300
301	foreach(temp, vars)
302	{
303		Node	   *node = (Node *) lfirst(temp);
304
305		if (IsA(node, Var))
306		{
307			Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
308			RelOptInfo *rel = find_base_rel(root, var->varno);
309			int			attno = var->varattno;
310
311			if (bms_is_subset(where_needed, rel->relids))
312				continue;
313			Assert(attno >= rel->min_attr && attno <= rel->max_attr);
314			attno -= rel->min_attr;
315			if (rel->attr_needed[attno] == NULL)
316			{
317				/*
318				 * Variable not yet requested, so add to rel's targetlist.
319				 *
320				 * The value available at the rel's scan level has not been
321				 * nulled by any outer join, so drop its varnullingrels.
322				 * (We'll put those back as we climb up the join tree.)
323				 */
324				var = copyObject(var);
325				var->varnullingrels = NULL;
326				rel->reltarget->exprs = lappend(rel->reltarget->exprs, var);
327				/* reltarget cost and width will be computed later */
328			}
329			rel->attr_needed[attno] = bms_add_members(rel->attr_needed[attno],
330													  where_needed);
331		}
332		else if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
333		{
334			PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
335			PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = find_placeholder_info(root, phv);
336
337			phinfo->ph_needed = bms_add_members(phinfo->ph_needed,
338												where_needed);
339		}
340		else
341			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d", (int) nodeTag(node));
342	}
343}
344
345/*
346 * add_vars_to_attr_needed
347 *	  This does a subset of what add_vars_to_targetlist does: it just
348 *	  updates attr_needed for Vars and ph_needed for PlaceHolderVars.
349 *	  We assume the Vars are already in their relations' targetlists.
350 *
351 *	  This is used to rebuild attr_needed/ph_needed sets after removal
352 *	  of a useless outer join.  The removed join clause might have been
353 *	  the only upper-level use of some other relation's Var, in which
354 *	  case we can reduce that Var's attr_needed and thereby possibly
355 *	  open the door to further join removals.  But we can't tell that
356 *	  without tedious reconstruction of the attr_needed data.
357 *
358 *	  Note that if a Var's attr_needed is successfully reduced to empty,
359 *	  it will still be in the relation's targetlist even though we do
360 *	  not really need the scan plan node to emit it.  The extra plan
361 *	  inefficiency seems tiny enough to not be worth spending planner
362 *	  cycles to get rid of it.
363 */
364void
365add_vars_to_attr_needed(PlannerInfo *root, List *vars,
366						Relids where_needed)
367{
368	ListCell   *temp;
369
370	Assert(!bms_is_empty(where_needed));
371
372	foreach(temp, vars)
373	{
374		Node	   *node = (Node *) lfirst(temp);
375
376		if (IsA(node, Var))
377		{
378			Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
379			RelOptInfo *rel = find_base_rel(root, var->varno);
380			int			attno = var->varattno;
381
382			if (bms_is_subset(where_needed, rel->relids))
383				continue;
384			Assert(attno >= rel->min_attr && attno <= rel->max_attr);
385			attno -= rel->min_attr;
386			rel->attr_needed[attno] = bms_add_members(rel->attr_needed[attno],
387													  where_needed);
388		}
389		else if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
390		{
391			PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
392			PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = find_placeholder_info(root, phv);
393
394			phinfo->ph_needed = bms_add_members(phinfo->ph_needed,
395												where_needed);
396		}
397		else
398			elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d", (int) nodeTag(node));
399	}
400}
401
402/*****************************************************************************
403 *
404 *	  GROUP BY
405 *
406 *****************************************************************************/
407
408/*
409 * remove_useless_groupby_columns
410 *		Remove any columns in the GROUP BY clause that are redundant due to
411 *		being functionally dependent on other GROUP BY columns.
412 *
413 * Since some other DBMSes do not allow references to ungrouped columns, it's
414 * not unusual to find all columns listed in GROUP BY even though listing the
415 * primary-key columns, or columns of a unique constraint would be sufficient.
416 * Deleting such excess columns avoids redundant sorting or hashing work, so
417 * it's worth doing.
418 *
419 * Relcache invalidations will ensure that cached plans become invalidated
420 * when the underlying supporting indexes are dropped or if a column's NOT
421 * NULL attribute is removed.
422 */
423void
424remove_useless_groupby_columns(PlannerInfo *root)
425{
426	Query	   *parse = root->parse;
427	Bitmapset **groupbyattnos;
428	List	  **groupbycols;
429	Bitmapset **surplusvars;
430	bool		tryremove = false;
431	ListCell   *lc;
432	int			relid;
433
434	/* No chance to do anything if there are less than two GROUP BY items */
435	if (list_length(root->processed_groupClause) < 2)
436		return;
437
438	/* Don't fiddle with the GROUP BY clause if the query has grouping sets */
439	if (parse->groupingSets)
440		return;
441
442	/*
443	 * Scan the GROUP BY clause to find GROUP BY items that are simple Vars.
444	 * Fill groupbyattnos[k] with a bitmapset of the column attnos of RTE k
445	 * that are GROUP BY items, and groupbycols[k] with a parallel list of
446	 * GroupByColInfo records.  We need the latter so that, when checking a
447	 * unique index against this rel's GROUP BY items, we can verify that the
448	 * index's notion of equality agrees with at least one GROUP BY item per
449	 * index column.
450	 */
451	groupbyattnos = (Bitmapset **) palloc0(sizeof(Bitmapset *) *
452										   (list_length(parse->rtable) + 1));
453	groupbycols = palloc0_array(List *, list_length(parse->rtable) + 1);
454	foreach(lc, root->processed_groupClause)
455	{
456		SortGroupClause *sgc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc);
457		TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sgc, parse->targetList);
458		Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
459		GroupByColInfo *info;
460
461		/*
462		 * Ignore non-Vars and Vars from other query levels.
463		 *
464		 * XXX in principle, stable expressions containing Vars could also be
465		 * removed, if all the Vars are functionally dependent on other GROUP
466		 * BY items.  But it's not clear that such cases occur often enough to
467		 * be worth troubling over.
468		 */
469		if (!IsA(var, Var) ||
470			var->varlevelsup > 0)
471			continue;
472
473		/* OK, remember we have this Var */
474		relid = var->varno;
475		Assert(relid <= list_length(parse->rtable));
476
477		/*
478		 * If this isn't the first column for this relation then we now have
479		 * multiple columns.  That means there might be some that can be
480		 * removed.
481		 */
482		tryremove |= !bms_is_empty(groupbyattnos[relid]);
483		groupbyattnos[relid] = bms_add_member(groupbyattnos[relid],
484											  var->varattno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
485
486		info = palloc(sizeof(GroupByColInfo));
487		info->attno = var->varattno;
488		info->eq_opfamilies = get_mergejoin_opfamilies(sgc->eqop);
489		info->coll = var->varcollid;
490		groupbycols[relid] = lappend(groupbycols[relid], info);
491	}
492
493	/*
494	 * No Vars or didn't find multiple Vars for any relation in the GROUP BY?
495	 * If so, nothing can be removed, so don't waste more effort trying.
496	 */
497	if (!tryremove)
498		return;
499
500	/*
501	 * Consider each relation and see if it is possible to remove some of its
502	 * Vars from GROUP BY.  For simplicity and speed, we do the actual removal
503	 * in a separate pass.  Here, we just fill surplusvars[k] with a bitmapset
504	 * of the column attnos of RTE k that are removable GROUP BY items.
505	 */
506	surplusvars = NULL;			/* don't allocate array unless required */
507	relid = 0;
508	foreach(lc, parse->rtable)
509	{
510		RangeTblEntry *rte = lfirst_node(RangeTblEntry, lc);
511		RelOptInfo *rel;
512		Bitmapset  *relattnos;
513		Bitmapset  *best_keycolumns = NULL;
514		int32		best_nkeycolumns = PG_INT32_MAX;
515
516		relid++;
517
518		/* Only plain relations could have primary-key constraints */
519		if (rte->rtekind != RTE_RELATION)
520			continue;
521
522		/*
523		 * We must skip inheritance parent tables as some of the child rels
524		 * may cause duplicate rows.  This cannot happen with partitioned
525		 * tables, however.
526		 */
527		if (rte->inh && rte->relkind != RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
528			continue;
529
530		/* Nothing to do unless this rel has multiple Vars in GROUP BY */
531		relattnos = groupbyattnos[relid];
532		if (bms_membership(relattnos) != BMS_MULTIPLE)
533			continue;
534
535		rel = root->simple_rel_array[relid];
536
537		/*
538		 * Now check each index for this relation to see if there are any with
539		 * columns which are a proper subset of the grouping columns for this
540		 * relation.
541		 */
542		foreach_node(IndexOptInfo, index, rel->indexlist)
543		{
544			Bitmapset  *ind_attnos;
545			bool		index_check_ok;
546
547			/*
548			 * Skip any non-unique and deferrable indexes.  Predicate indexes
549			 * have not been checked yet, so we must skip those too as the
550			 * predOK check that's done later might fail.
551			 */
552			if (!index->unique || !index->immediate || index->indpred != NIL)
553				continue;
554
555			/* For simplicity, we currently don't support expression indexes */
556			if (index->indexprs != NIL)
557				continue;
558
559			ind_attnos = NULL;
560			index_check_ok = true;
561			for (int i = 0; i < index->nkeycolumns; i++)
562			{
563				AttrNumber	indkey_attno = index->indexkeys[i];
564				Oid			indkey_opfamily = index->opfamily[i];
565				Oid			indkey_coll = index->indexcollations[i];
566				ListCell   *lc2;
567
568				/*
569				 * We must insist that the index columns are all defined NOT
570				 * NULL otherwise duplicate NULLs could exist.  However, we
571				 * can relax this check when the index is defined with NULLS
572				 * NOT DISTINCT as there can only be 1 NULL row, therefore
573				 * functional dependency on the unique columns is maintained,
574				 * despite the NULL.
575				 */
576				if (!index->nullsnotdistinct &&
577					!bms_is_member(indkey_attno, rel->notnullattnums))
578				{
579					index_check_ok = false;
580					break;
581				}
582
583				/*
584				 * The index proves uniqueness only under its own opfamily and
585				 * collation.  Require some GROUP BY item on this column to
586				 * use a compatible eqop and collation, the same check
587				 * relation_has_unique_index_for() applies to join clauses.
588				 */
589				foreach(lc2, groupbycols[relid])
590				{
591					GroupByColInfo *info = (GroupByColInfo *) lfirst(lc2);
592
593					if (info->attno != indkey_attno)
594						continue;
595					if (list_member_oid(info->eq_opfamilies, indkey_opfamily) &&
596						collations_agree_on_equality(indkey_coll, info->coll))
597						break;
598				}
599				if (lc2 == NULL)
600				{
601					index_check_ok = false;
602					break;
603				}
604
605				ind_attnos =
606					bms_add_member(ind_attnos,
607								   indkey_attno -
608								   FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber);
609			}
610
611			if (!index_check_ok)
612				continue;
613
614			/*
615			 * Skip any indexes where the indexed columns aren't a proper
616			 * subset of the GROUP BY.
617			 */
618			if (bms_subset_compare(ind_attnos, relattnos) != BMS_SUBSET1)
619				continue;
620
621			/*
622			 * Record the attribute numbers from the index with the fewest
623			 * columns.  This allows the largest number of columns to be
624			 * removed from the GROUP BY clause.  In the future, we may wish
625			 * to consider using the narrowest set of columns and looking at
626			 * pg_statistic.stawidth as it might be better to use an index
627			 * with, say two INT4s, rather than, say, one long varlena column.
628			 */
629			if (index->nkeycolumns < best_nkeycolumns)
630			{
631				best_keycolumns = ind_attnos;
632				best_nkeycolumns = index->nkeycolumns;
633			}
634		}
635
636		/* Did we find a suitable index? */
637		if (!bms_is_empty(best_keycolumns))
638		{
639			/*
640			 * To easily remember whether we've found anything to do, we don't
641			 * allocate the surplusvars[] array until we find something.
642			 */
643			if (surplusvars == NULL)
644				surplusvars = (Bitmapset **) palloc0(sizeof(Bitmapset *) *
645													 (list_length(parse->rtable) + 1));
646
647			/* Remember the attnos of the removable columns */
648			surplusvars[relid] = bms_difference(relattnos, best_keycolumns);
649		}
650	}
651
652	/*
653	 * If we found any surplus Vars, build a new GROUP BY clause without them.
654	 * (Note: this may leave some TLEs with unreferenced ressortgroupref
655	 * markings, but that's harmless.)
656	 */
657	if (surplusvars != NULL)
658	{
659		List	   *new_groupby = NIL;
660
661		foreach(lc, root->processed_groupClause)
662		{
663			SortGroupClause *sgc = lfirst_node(SortGroupClause, lc);
664			TargetEntry *tle = get_sortgroupclause_tle(sgc, parse->targetList);
665			Var		   *var = (Var *) tle->expr;
666
667			/*
668			 * New list must include non-Vars, outer Vars, and anything not
669			 * marked as surplus.
670			 */
671			if (!IsA(var, Var) ||
672				var->varlevelsup > 0 ||
673				!bms_is_member(var->varattno - FirstLowInvalidHeapAttributeNumber,
674							   surplusvars[var->varno]))
675				new_groupby = lappend(new_groupby, sgc);
676		}
677
678		root->processed_groupClause = new_groupby;
679	}
680}
681
682/*****************************************************************************
683 *
684 *	  LATERAL REFERENCES
685 *
686 *****************************************************************************/
687
688/*
689 * find_lateral_references
690 *	  For each LATERAL subquery, extract all its references to Vars and
691 *	  PlaceHolderVars of the current query level, and make sure those values
692 *	  will be available for evaluation of the subquery.
693 *
694 * While later planning steps ensure that the Var/PHV source rels are on the
695 * outside of nestloops relative to the LATERAL subquery, we also need to
696 * ensure that the Vars/PHVs propagate up to the nestloop join level; this
697 * means setting suitable where_needed values for them.
698 *
699 * Note that this only deals with lateral references in unflattened LATERAL
700 * subqueries.  When we flatten a LATERAL subquery, its lateral references
701 * become plain Vars in the parent query, but they may have to be wrapped in
702 * PlaceHolderVars if they need to be forced NULL by outer joins that don't
703 * also null the LATERAL subquery.  That's all handled elsewhere.
704 *
705 * This has to run before deconstruct_jointree, since it might result in
706 * creation of PlaceHolderInfos.
707 */
708void
709find_lateral_references(PlannerInfo *root)
710{
711	Index		rti;
712
713	/* We need do nothing if the query contains no LATERAL RTEs */
714	if (!root->hasLateralRTEs)
715		return;
716
717	/*
718	 * Examine all baserels (the rel array has been set up by now).
719	 */
720	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
721	{
722		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
723
724		/* there may be empty slots corresponding to non-baserel RTEs */
725		if (brel == NULL)
726			continue;
727
728		Assert(brel->relid == rti); /* sanity check on array */
729
730		/*
731		 * This bit is less obvious than it might look.  We ignore appendrel
732		 * otherrels and consider only their parent baserels.  In a case where
733		 * a LATERAL-containing UNION ALL subquery was pulled up, it is the
734		 * otherrel that is actually going to be in the plan.  However, we
735		 * want to mark all its lateral references as needed by the parent,
736		 * because it is the parent's relid that will be used for join
737		 * planning purposes.  And the parent's RTE will contain all the
738		 * lateral references we need to know, since the pulled-up member is
739		 * nothing but a copy of parts of the original RTE's subquery.  We
740		 * could visit the parent's children instead and transform their
741		 * references back to the parent's relid, but it would be much more
742		 * complicated for no real gain.  (Important here is that the child
743		 * members have not yet received any processing beyond being pulled
744		 * up.)  Similarly, in appendrels created by inheritance expansion,
745		 * it's sufficient to look at the parent relation.
746		 */
747
748		/* ignore RTEs that are "other rels" */
749		if (brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
750			continue;
751
752		extract_lateral_references(root, brel, rti);
753	}
754}
755
756static void
757extract_lateral_references(PlannerInfo *root, RelOptInfo *brel, Index rtindex)
758{
759	RangeTblEntry *rte = root->simple_rte_array[rtindex];
760	List	   *vars;
761	List	   *newvars;
762	Relids		where_needed;
763	ListCell   *lc;
764
765	/* No cross-references are possible if it's not LATERAL */
766	if (!rte->lateral)
767		return;
768
769	/* Fetch the appropriate variables */
770	if (rte->rtekind == RTE_RELATION)
771		vars = pull_vars_of_level((Node *) rte->tablesample, 0);
772	else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_SUBQUERY)
773		vars = pull_vars_of_level((Node *) rte->subquery, 1);
774	else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_FUNCTION)
775		vars = pull_vars_of_level((Node *) rte->functions, 0);
776	else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_TABLEFUNC)
777		vars = pull_vars_of_level((Node *) rte->tablefunc, 0);
778	else if (rte->rtekind == RTE_VALUES)
779		vars = pull_vars_of_level((Node *) rte->values_lists, 0);
780	else
781	{
782		Assert(false);
783		return;					/* keep compiler quiet */
784	}
785
786	if (vars == NIL)
787		return;					/* nothing to do */
788
789	/* Copy each Var (or PlaceHolderVar) and adjust it to match our level */
790	newvars = NIL;
791	foreach(lc, vars)
792	{
793		Node	   *node = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
794
795		node = copyObject(node);
796		if (IsA(node, Var))
797		{
798			Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
799
800			/* Adjustment is easy since it's just one node */
801			var->varlevelsup = 0;
802		}
803		else if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
804		{
805			PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
806			int			levelsup = phv->phlevelsup;
807
808			/* Have to work harder to adjust the contained expression too */
809			if (levelsup != 0)
810				IncrementVarSublevelsUp(node, -levelsup, 0);
811
812			/*
813			 * If we pulled the PHV out of a subquery RTE, its expression
814			 * needs to be preprocessed.  subquery_planner() already did this
815			 * for level-zero PHVs in function and values RTEs, though.
816			 */
817			if (levelsup > 0)
818				phv->phexpr = preprocess_phv_expression(root, phv->phexpr);
819		}
820		else
821			Assert(false);
822		newvars = lappend(newvars, node);
823	}
824
825	list_free(vars);
826
827	/*
828	 * We mark the Vars as being "needed" at the LATERAL RTE.  This is a bit
829	 * of a cheat: a more formal approach would be to mark each one as needed
830	 * at the join of the LATERAL RTE with its source RTE.  But it will work,
831	 * and it's much less tedious than computing a separate where_needed for
832	 * each Var.
833	 */
834	where_needed = bms_make_singleton(rtindex);
835
836	/*
837	 * Push Vars into their source relations' targetlists, and PHVs into
838	 * root->placeholder_list.
839	 */
840	add_vars_to_targetlist(root, newvars, where_needed);
841
842	/*
843	 * Remember the lateral references for rebuild_lateral_attr_needed and
844	 * create_lateral_join_info.
845	 */
846	brel->lateral_vars = newvars;
847}
848
849/*
850 * rebuild_lateral_attr_needed
851 *	  Put back attr_needed bits for Vars/PHVs needed for lateral references.
852 *
853 * This is used to rebuild attr_needed/ph_needed sets after removal of a
854 * useless outer join.  It should match what find_lateral_references did,
855 * except that we call add_vars_to_attr_needed not add_vars_to_targetlist.
856 */
857void
858rebuild_lateral_attr_needed(PlannerInfo *root)
859{
860	Index		rti;
861
862	/* We need do nothing if the query contains no LATERAL RTEs */
863	if (!root->hasLateralRTEs)
864		return;
865
866	/* Examine the same baserels that find_lateral_references did */
867	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
868	{
869		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
870		Relids		where_needed;
871
872		if (brel == NULL)
873			continue;
874		if (brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
875			continue;
876
877		/*
878		 * We don't need to repeat all of extract_lateral_references, since it
879		 * kindly saved the extracted Vars/PHVs in lateral_vars.
880		 */
881		if (brel->lateral_vars == NIL)
882			continue;
883
884		where_needed = bms_make_singleton(rti);
885
886		add_vars_to_attr_needed(root, brel->lateral_vars, where_needed);
887	}
888}
889
890/*
891 * create_lateral_join_info
892 *	  Fill in the per-base-relation direct_lateral_relids, lateral_relids
893 *	  and lateral_referencers sets.
894 */
895void
896create_lateral_join_info(PlannerInfo *root)
897{
898	bool		found_laterals = false;
899	Index		rti;
900	ListCell   *lc;
901
902	/* We need do nothing if the query contains no LATERAL RTEs */
903	if (!root->hasLateralRTEs)
904		return;
905
906	/* We'll need to have the ph_eval_at values for PlaceHolderVars */
907	Assert(root->placeholdersFrozen);
908
909	/*
910	 * Examine all baserels (the rel array has been set up by now).
911	 */
912	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
913	{
914		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
915		Relids		lateral_relids;
916
917		/* there may be empty slots corresponding to non-baserel RTEs */
918		if (brel == NULL)
919			continue;
920
921		Assert(brel->relid == rti); /* sanity check on array */
922
923		/* ignore RTEs that are "other rels" */
924		if (brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
925			continue;
926
927		lateral_relids = NULL;
928
929		/* consider each laterally-referenced Var or PHV */
930		foreach(lc, brel->lateral_vars)
931		{
932			Node	   *node = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
933
934			if (IsA(node, Var))
935			{
936				Var		   *var = (Var *) node;
937
938				found_laterals = true;
939				lateral_relids = bms_add_member(lateral_relids,
940												var->varno);
941			}
942			else if (IsA(node, PlaceHolderVar))
943			{
944				PlaceHolderVar *phv = (PlaceHolderVar *) node;
945				PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = find_placeholder_info(root, phv);
946
947				found_laterals = true;
948				lateral_relids = bms_add_members(lateral_relids,
949												 phinfo->ph_eval_at);
950			}
951			else
952				Assert(false);
953		}
954
955		/* We now have all the simple lateral refs from this rel */
956		brel->direct_lateral_relids = lateral_relids;
957		brel->lateral_relids = bms_copy(lateral_relids);
958	}
959
960	/*
961	 * Now check for lateral references within PlaceHolderVars, and mark their
962	 * eval_at rels as having lateral references to the source rels.
963	 *
964	 * For a PHV that is due to be evaluated at a baserel, mark its source(s)
965	 * as direct lateral dependencies of the baserel (adding onto the ones
966	 * recorded above).  If it's due to be evaluated at a join, mark its
967	 * source(s) as indirect lateral dependencies of each baserel in the join,
968	 * ie put them into lateral_relids but not direct_lateral_relids.  This is
969	 * appropriate because we can't put any such baserel on the outside of a
970	 * join to one of the PHV's lateral dependencies, but on the other hand we
971	 * also can't yet join it directly to the dependency.
972	 */
973	foreach(lc, root->placeholder_list)
974	{
975		PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = (PlaceHolderInfo *) lfirst(lc);
976		Relids		eval_at = phinfo->ph_eval_at;
977		Relids		lateral_refs;
978		int			varno;
979
980		if (phinfo->ph_lateral == NULL)
981			continue;			/* PHV is uninteresting if no lateral refs */
982
983		found_laterals = true;
984
985		/*
986		 * Include only baserels not outer joins in the evaluation sites'
987		 * lateral relids.  This avoids problems when outer join order gets
988		 * rearranged, and it should still ensure that the lateral values are
989		 * available when needed.
990		 */
991		lateral_refs = bms_intersect(phinfo->ph_lateral, root->all_baserels);
992		Assert(!bms_is_empty(lateral_refs));
993
994		if (bms_get_singleton_member(eval_at, &varno))
995		{
996			/* Evaluation site is a baserel */
997			RelOptInfo *brel = find_base_rel(root, varno);
998
999			brel->direct_lateral_relids =
1000				bms_add_members(brel->direct_lateral_relids,
1001								lateral_refs);
1002			brel->lateral_relids =
1003				bms_add_members(brel->lateral_relids,
1004								lateral_refs);
1005		}
1006		else
1007		{
1008			/* Evaluation site is a join */
1009			varno = -1;
1010			while ((varno = bms_next_member(eval_at, varno)) >= 0)
1011			{
1012				RelOptInfo *brel = find_base_rel_ignore_join(root, varno);
1013
1014				if (brel == NULL)
1015					continue;	/* ignore outer joins in eval_at */
1016				brel->lateral_relids = bms_add_members(brel->lateral_relids,
1017													   lateral_refs);
1018			}
1019		}
1020	}
1021
1022	/*
1023	 * If we found no actual lateral references, we're done; but reset the
1024	 * hasLateralRTEs flag to avoid useless work later.
1025	 */
1026	if (!found_laterals)
1027	{
1028		root->hasLateralRTEs = false;
1029		return;
1030	}
1031
1032	/*
1033	 * Calculate the transitive closure of the lateral_relids sets, so that
1034	 * they describe both direct and indirect lateral references.  If relation
1035	 * X references Y laterally, and Y references Z laterally, then we will
1036	 * have to scan X on the inside of a nestloop with Z, so for all intents
1037	 * and purposes X is laterally dependent on Z too.
1038	 *
1039	 * This code is essentially Warshall's algorithm for transitive closure.
1040	 * The outer loop considers each baserel, and propagates its lateral
1041	 * dependencies to those baserels that have a lateral dependency on it.
1042	 */
1043	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
1044	{
1045		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
1046		Relids		outer_lateral_relids;
1047		Index		rti2;
1048
1049		if (brel == NULL || brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
1050			continue;
1051
1052		/* need not consider baserel further if it has no lateral refs */
1053		outer_lateral_relids = brel->lateral_relids;
1054		if (outer_lateral_relids == NULL)
1055			continue;
1056
1057		/* else scan all baserels */
1058		for (rti2 = 1; rti2 < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti2++)
1059		{
1060			RelOptInfo *brel2 = root->simple_rel_array[rti2];
1061
1062			if (brel2 == NULL || brel2->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
1063				continue;
1064
1065			/* if brel2 has lateral ref to brel, propagate brel's refs */
1066			if (bms_is_member(rti, brel2->lateral_relids))
1067				brel2->lateral_relids = bms_add_members(brel2->lateral_relids,
1068														outer_lateral_relids);
1069		}
1070	}
1071
1072	/*
1073	 * Now that we've identified all lateral references, mark each baserel
1074	 * with the set of relids of rels that reference it laterally (possibly
1075	 * indirectly) --- that is, the inverse mapping of lateral_relids.
1076	 */
1077	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
1078	{
1079		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
1080		Relids		lateral_relids;
1081		int			rti2;
1082
1083		if (brel == NULL || brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
1084			continue;
1085
1086		/* Nothing to do at rels with no lateral refs */
1087		lateral_relids = brel->lateral_relids;
1088		if (bms_is_empty(lateral_relids))
1089			continue;
1090
1091		/* No rel should have a lateral dependency on itself */
1092		Assert(!bms_is_member(rti, lateral_relids));
1093
1094		/* Mark this rel's referencees */
1095		rti2 = -1;
1096		while ((rti2 = bms_next_member(lateral_relids, rti2)) >= 0)
1097		{
1098			RelOptInfo *brel2 = root->simple_rel_array[rti2];
1099
1100			if (brel2 == NULL)
1101				continue;		/* must be an OJ */
1102
1103			Assert(brel2->reloptkind == RELOPT_BASEREL);
1104			brel2->lateral_referencers =
1105				bms_add_member(brel2->lateral_referencers, rti);
1106		}
1107	}
1108}
1109
1110
1111/*****************************************************************************
1112 *
1113 *	  JOIN TREE PROCESSING
1114 *
1115 *****************************************************************************/
1116
1117/*
1118 * deconstruct_jointree
1119 *	  Recursively scan the query's join tree for WHERE and JOIN/ON qual
1120 *	  clauses, and add these to the appropriate restrictinfo and joininfo
1121 *	  lists belonging to base RelOptInfos.  Also, add SpecialJoinInfo nodes
1122 *	  to root->join_info_list for any outer joins appearing in the query tree.
1123 *	  Return a "joinlist" data structure showing the join order decisions
1124 *	  that need to be made by make_one_rel().
1125 *
1126 * The "joinlist" result is a list of items that are either RangeTblRef
1127 * jointree nodes or sub-joinlists.  All the items at the same level of
1128 * joinlist must be joined in an order to be determined by make_one_rel()
1129 * (note that legal orders may be constrained by SpecialJoinInfo nodes).
1130 * A sub-joinlist represents a subproblem to be planned separately. Currently
1131 * sub-joinlists arise only from FULL OUTER JOIN or when collapsing of
1132 * subproblems is stopped by join_collapse_limit or from_collapse_limit.
1133 */
1134List *
1135deconstruct_jointree(PlannerInfo *root)
1136{
1137	List	   *result;
1138	JoinDomain *top_jdomain;
1139	List	   *item_list = NIL;
1140	ListCell   *lc;
1141
1142	/*
1143	 * After this point, no more PlaceHolderInfos may be made, because
1144	 * make_outerjoininfo requires all active placeholders to be present in
1145	 * root->placeholder_list while we crawl up the join tree.
1146	 */
1147	root->placeholdersFrozen = true;
1148
1149	/* Fetch the already-created top-level join domain for the query */
1150	top_jdomain = linitial_node(JoinDomain, root->join_domains);
1151	top_jdomain->jd_relids = NULL;	/* filled during deconstruct_recurse */
1152
1153	/* Start recursion at top of jointree */
1154	Assert(root->parse->jointree != NULL &&
1155		   IsA(root->parse->jointree, FromExpr));
1156
1157	/* These are filled as we scan the jointree */
1158	root->all_baserels = NULL;
1159	root->outer_join_rels = NULL;
1160
1161	/* Perform the initial scan of the jointree */
1162	result = deconstruct_recurse(root, (Node *) root->parse->jointree,
1163								 top_jdomain, NULL,
1164								 &item_list);
1165
1166	/* Now we can form the value of all_query_rels, too */
1167	root->all_query_rels = bms_union(root->all_baserels, root->outer_join_rels);
1168
1169	/* ... which should match what we computed for the top join domain */
1170	Assert(bms_equal(root->all_query_rels, top_jdomain->jd_relids));
1171
1172	/* Now scan all the jointree nodes again, and distribute quals */
1173	foreach(lc, item_list)
1174	{
1175		JoinTreeItem *jtitem = (JoinTreeItem *) lfirst(lc);
1176
1177		deconstruct_distribute(root, jtitem);
1178	}
1179
1180	/*
1181	 * If there were any special joins then we may have some postponed LEFT
1182	 * JOIN clauses to deal with.
1183	 */
1184	if (root->join_info_list)
1185	{
1186		foreach(lc, item_list)
1187		{
1188			JoinTreeItem *jtitem = (JoinTreeItem *) lfirst(lc);
1189
1190			if (jtitem->oj_joinclauses != NIL)
1191				deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals(root, item_list, jtitem);
1192		}
1193	}
1194
1195	/* Don't need the JoinTreeItems any more */
1196	list_free_deep(item_list);
1197
1198	return result;
1199}
1200
1201/*
1202 * deconstruct_recurse
1203 *	  One recursion level of deconstruct_jointree's initial jointree scan.
1204 *
1205 * jtnode is the jointree node to examine, and parent_domain is the
1206 * enclosing join domain.  (We must add all base+OJ relids appearing
1207 * here or below to parent_domain.)  parent_jtitem is the JoinTreeItem
1208 * for the parent jointree node, or NULL at the top of the recursion.
1209 *
1210 * item_list is an in/out parameter: we add a JoinTreeItem struct to
1211 * that list for each jointree node, in depth-first traversal order.
1212 * (Hence, after each call, the last list item corresponds to its jtnode.)
1213 *
1214 * Return value is the appropriate joinlist for this jointree node.
1215 */
1216static List *
1217deconstruct_recurse(PlannerInfo *root, Node *jtnode,
1218					JoinDomain *parent_domain,
1219					JoinTreeItem *parent_jtitem,
1220					List **item_list)
1221{
1222	List	   *joinlist;
1223	JoinTreeItem *jtitem;
1224
1225	Assert(jtnode != NULL);
1226
1227	/* Make the new JoinTreeItem, but don't add it to item_list yet */
1228	jtitem = palloc0_object(JoinTreeItem);
1229	jtitem->jtnode = jtnode;
1230	jtitem->jti_parent = parent_jtitem;
1231
1232	if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
1233	{
1234		int			varno = ((RangeTblRef *) jtnode)->rtindex;
1235
1236		/* Fill all_baserels as we encounter baserel jointree nodes */
1237		root->all_baserels = bms_add_member(root->all_baserels, varno);
1238		/* This node belongs to parent_domain */
1239		jtitem->jdomain = parent_domain;
1240		parent_domain->jd_relids = bms_add_member(parent_domain->jd_relids,
1241												  varno);
1242		/* qualscope is just the one RTE */
1243		jtitem->qualscope = bms_make_singleton(varno);
1244		/* A single baserel does not create an inner join */
1245		jtitem->inner_join_rels = NULL;
1246		joinlist = list_make1(jtnode);
1247	}
1248	else if (IsA(jtnode, FromExpr))
1249	{
1250		FromExpr   *f = (FromExpr *) jtnode;
1251		int			remaining;
1252		ListCell   *l;
1253
1254		/* This node belongs to parent_domain, as do its children */
1255		jtitem->jdomain = parent_domain;
1256
1257		/*
1258		 * Recurse to handle child nodes, and compute output joinlist.  We
1259		 * collapse subproblems into a single joinlist whenever the resulting
1260		 * joinlist wouldn't exceed from_collapse_limit members.  Also, always
1261		 * collapse one-element subproblems, since that won't lengthen the
1262		 * joinlist anyway.
1263		 */
1264		jtitem->qualscope = NULL;
1265		jtitem->inner_join_rels = NULL;
1266		joinlist = NIL;
1267		remaining = list_length(f->fromlist);
1268		foreach(l, f->fromlist)
1269		{
1270			JoinTreeItem *sub_item;
1271			List	   *sub_joinlist;
1272			int			sub_members;
1273
1274			sub_joinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, lfirst(l),
1275											   parent_domain,
1276											   jtitem,
1277											   item_list);
1278			sub_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1279			jtitem->qualscope = bms_add_members(jtitem->qualscope,
1280												sub_item->qualscope);
1281			jtitem->inner_join_rels = sub_item->inner_join_rels;
1282			sub_members = list_length(sub_joinlist);
1283			remaining--;
1284			if (sub_members <= 1 ||
1285				list_length(joinlist) + sub_members + remaining <= from_collapse_limit)
1286				joinlist = list_concat(joinlist, sub_joinlist);
1287			else
1288				joinlist = lappend(joinlist, sub_joinlist);
1289		}
1290
1291		/*
1292		 * A FROM with more than one list element is an inner join subsuming
1293		 * all below it, so we should report inner_join_rels = qualscope. If
1294		 * there was exactly one element, we should (and already did) report
1295		 * whatever its inner_join_rels were.  If there were no elements (is
1296		 * that still possible?) the initialization before the loop fixed it.
1297		 */
1298		if (list_length(f->fromlist) > 1)
1299			jtitem->inner_join_rels = jtitem->qualscope;
1300	}
1301	else if (IsA(jtnode, JoinExpr))
1302	{
1303		JoinExpr   *j = (JoinExpr *) jtnode;
1304		JoinDomain *child_domain,
1305				   *fj_domain;
1306		JoinTreeItem *left_item,
1307				   *right_item;
1308		List	   *leftjoinlist,
1309				   *rightjoinlist;
1310
1311		switch (j->jointype)
1312		{
1313			case JOIN_INNER:
1314				/* This node belongs to parent_domain, as do its children */
1315				jtitem->jdomain = parent_domain;
1316				/* Recurse */
1317				leftjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->larg,
1318												   parent_domain,
1319												   jtitem,
1320												   item_list);
1321				left_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1322				rightjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->rarg,
1323													parent_domain,
1324													jtitem,
1325													item_list);
1326				right_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1327				/* Compute qualscope etc */
1328				jtitem->qualscope = bms_union(left_item->qualscope,
1329											  right_item->qualscope);
1330				jtitem->inner_join_rels = jtitem->qualscope;
1331				jtitem->left_rels = left_item->qualscope;
1332				jtitem->right_rels = right_item->qualscope;
1333				/* Inner join adds no restrictions for quals */
1334				jtitem->nonnullable_rels = NULL;
1335				break;
1336			case JOIN_LEFT:
1337			case JOIN_ANTI:
1338				/* Make new join domain for my quals and the RHS */
1339				child_domain = makeNode(JoinDomain);
1340				child_domain->jd_relids = NULL; /* filled by recursion */
1341				root->join_domains = lappend(root->join_domains, child_domain);
1342				jtitem->jdomain = child_domain;
1343				/* Recurse */
1344				leftjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->larg,
1345												   parent_domain,
1346												   jtitem,
1347												   item_list);
1348				left_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1349				rightjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->rarg,
1350													child_domain,
1351													jtitem,
1352													item_list);
1353				right_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1354				/* Compute join domain contents, qualscope etc */
1355				parent_domain->jd_relids =
1356					bms_add_members(parent_domain->jd_relids,
1357									child_domain->jd_relids);
1358				jtitem->qualscope = bms_union(left_item->qualscope,
1359											  right_item->qualscope);
1360				/* caution: ANTI join derived from SEMI will lack rtindex */
1361				if (j->rtindex != 0)
1362				{
1363					parent_domain->jd_relids =
1364						bms_add_member(parent_domain->jd_relids,
1365									   j->rtindex);
1366					jtitem->qualscope = bms_add_member(jtitem->qualscope,
1367													   j->rtindex);
1368					root->outer_join_rels = bms_add_member(root->outer_join_rels,
1369														   j->rtindex);
1370					mark_rels_nulled_by_join(root, j->rtindex,
1371											 right_item->qualscope);
1372				}
1373				jtitem->inner_join_rels = bms_union(left_item->inner_join_rels,
1374													right_item->inner_join_rels);
1375				jtitem->left_rels = left_item->qualscope;
1376				jtitem->right_rels = right_item->qualscope;
1377				jtitem->nonnullable_rels = left_item->qualscope;
1378				break;
1379			case JOIN_SEMI:
1380				/* This node belongs to parent_domain, as do its children */
1381				jtitem->jdomain = parent_domain;
1382				/* Recurse */
1383				leftjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->larg,
1384												   parent_domain,
1385												   jtitem,
1386												   item_list);
1387				left_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1388				rightjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->rarg,
1389													parent_domain,
1390													jtitem,
1391													item_list);
1392				right_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1393				/* Compute qualscope etc */
1394				jtitem->qualscope = bms_union(left_item->qualscope,
1395											  right_item->qualscope);
1396				/* SEMI join never has rtindex, so don't add to anything */
1397				Assert(j->rtindex == 0);
1398				jtitem->inner_join_rels = bms_union(left_item->inner_join_rels,
1399													right_item->inner_join_rels);
1400				jtitem->left_rels = left_item->qualscope;
1401				jtitem->right_rels = right_item->qualscope;
1402				/* Semi join adds no restrictions for quals */
1403				jtitem->nonnullable_rels = NULL;
1404				break;
1405			case JOIN_FULL:
1406				/* The FULL JOIN's quals need their very own domain */
1407				fj_domain = makeNode(JoinDomain);
1408				root->join_domains = lappend(root->join_domains, fj_domain);
1409				jtitem->jdomain = fj_domain;
1410				/* Recurse, giving each side its own join domain */
1411				child_domain = makeNode(JoinDomain);
1412				child_domain->jd_relids = NULL; /* filled by recursion */
1413				root->join_domains = lappend(root->join_domains, child_domain);
1414				leftjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->larg,
1415												   child_domain,
1416												   jtitem,
1417												   item_list);
1418				left_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1419				fj_domain->jd_relids = bms_copy(child_domain->jd_relids);
1420				child_domain = makeNode(JoinDomain);
1421				child_domain->jd_relids = NULL; /* filled by recursion */
1422				root->join_domains = lappend(root->join_domains, child_domain);
1423				rightjoinlist = deconstruct_recurse(root, j->rarg,
1424													child_domain,
1425													jtitem,
1426													item_list);
1427				right_item = (JoinTreeItem *) llast(*item_list);
1428				/* Compute qualscope etc */
1429				fj_domain->jd_relids = bms_add_members(fj_domain->jd_relids,
1430													   child_domain->jd_relids);
1431				parent_domain->jd_relids = bms_add_members(parent_domain->jd_relids,
1432														   fj_domain->jd_relids);
1433				jtitem->qualscope = bms_union(left_item->qualscope,
1434											  right_item->qualscope);
1435				Assert(j->rtindex != 0);
1436				parent_domain->jd_relids = bms_add_member(parent_domain->jd_relids,
1437														  j->rtindex);
1438				jtitem->qualscope = bms_add_member(jtitem->qualscope,
1439												   j->rtindex);
1440				root->outer_join_rels = bms_add_member(root->outer_join_rels,
1441													   j->rtindex);
1442				mark_rels_nulled_by_join(root, j->rtindex,
1443										 left_item->qualscope);
1444				mark_rels_nulled_by_join(root, j->rtindex,
1445										 right_item->qualscope);
1446				jtitem->inner_join_rels = bms_union(left_item->inner_join_rels,
1447													right_item->inner_join_rels);
1448				jtitem->left_rels = left_item->qualscope;
1449				jtitem->right_rels = right_item->qualscope;
1450				/* each side is both outer and inner */
1451				jtitem->nonnullable_rels = jtitem->qualscope;
1452				break;
1453			default:
1454				/* JOIN_RIGHT was eliminated during reduce_outer_joins() */
1455				elog(ERROR, "unrecognized join type: %d",
1456					 (int) j->jointype);
1457				leftjoinlist = rightjoinlist = NIL; /* keep compiler quiet */
1458				break;
1459		}
1460
1461		/*
1462		 * Compute the output joinlist.  We fold subproblems together except
1463		 * at a FULL JOIN or where join_collapse_limit would be exceeded.
1464		 */
1465		if (j->jointype == JOIN_FULL)
1466		{
1467			/* force the join order exactly at this node */
1468			joinlist = list_make1(list_make2(leftjoinlist, rightjoinlist));
1469		}
1470		else if (list_length(leftjoinlist) + list_length(rightjoinlist) <=
1471				 join_collapse_limit)
1472		{
1473			/* OK to combine subproblems */
1474			joinlist = list_concat(leftjoinlist, rightjoinlist);
1475		}
1476		else
1477		{
1478			/* can't combine, but needn't force join order above here */
1479			Node	   *leftpart,
1480					   *rightpart;
1481
1482			/* avoid creating useless 1-element sublists */
1483			if (list_length(leftjoinlist) == 1)
1484				leftpart = (Node *) linitial(leftjoinlist);
1485			else
1486				leftpart = (Node *) leftjoinlist;
1487			if (list_length(rightjoinlist) == 1)
1488				rightpart = (Node *) linitial(rightjoinlist);
1489			else
1490				rightpart = (Node *) rightjoinlist;
1491			joinlist = list_make2(leftpart, rightpart);
1492		}
1493	}
1494	else
1495	{
1496		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
1497			 (int) nodeTag(jtnode));
1498		joinlist = NIL;			/* keep compiler quiet */
1499	}
1500
1501	/* Finally, we can add the new JoinTreeItem to item_list */
1502	*item_list = lappend(*item_list, jtitem);
1503
1504	return joinlist;
1505}
1506
1507/*
1508 * deconstruct_distribute
1509 *	  Process one jointree node in phase 2 of deconstruct_jointree processing.
1510 *
1511 * Distribute quals of the node to appropriate restriction and join lists.
1512 * In addition, entries will be added to root->join_info_list for outer joins.
1513 */
1514static void
1515deconstruct_distribute(PlannerInfo *root, JoinTreeItem *jtitem)
1516{
1517	Node	   *jtnode = jtitem->jtnode;
1518
1519	if (IsA(jtnode, RangeTblRef))
1520	{
1521		int			varno = ((RangeTblRef *) jtnode)->rtindex;
1522
1523		/* Deal with any securityQuals attached to the RTE */
1524		if (root->qual_security_level > 0)
1525			process_security_barrier_quals(root,
1526										   varno,
1527										   jtitem);
1528	}
1529	else if (IsA(jtnode, FromExpr))
1530	{
1531		FromExpr   *f = (FromExpr *) jtnode;
1532
1533		/*
1534		 * Process any lateral-referencing quals that were postponed to this
1535		 * level by children.
1536		 */
1537		distribute_quals_to_rels(root, jtitem->lateral_clauses,
1538								 jtitem,
1539								 NULL,
1540								 root->qual_security_level,
1541								 jtitem->qualscope,
1542								 NULL, NULL, NULL,
1543								 true, false, false,
1544								 NULL);
1545
1546		/*
1547		 * Now process the top-level quals.
1548		 */
1549		distribute_quals_to_rels(root, (List *) f->quals,
1550								 jtitem,
1551								 NULL,
1552								 root->qual_security_level,
1553								 jtitem->qualscope,
1554								 NULL, NULL, NULL,
1555								 true, false, false,
1556								 NULL);
1557	}
1558	else if (IsA(jtnode, JoinExpr))
1559	{
1560		JoinExpr   *j = (JoinExpr *) jtnode;
1561		Relids		ojscope;
1562		List	   *my_quals;
1563		SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo;
1564		List	  **postponed_oj_qual_list;
1565
1566		/*
1567		 * Include lateral-referencing quals postponed from children in
1568		 * my_quals, so that they'll be handled properly in
1569		 * make_outerjoininfo.  (This is destructive to
1570		 * jtitem->lateral_clauses, but we won't use that again.)
1571		 */
1572		my_quals = list_concat(jtitem->lateral_clauses,
1573							   (List *) j->quals);
1574
1575		/*
1576		 * For an OJ, form the SpecialJoinInfo now, so that we can pass it to
1577		 * distribute_qual_to_rels.  We must compute its ojscope too.
1578		 *
1579		 * Semijoins are a bit of a hybrid: we build a SpecialJoinInfo, but we
1580		 * want ojscope = NULL for distribute_qual_to_rels.
1581		 */
1582		if (j->jointype != JOIN_INNER)
1583		{
1584			sjinfo = make_outerjoininfo(root,
1585										jtitem->left_rels,
1586										jtitem->right_rels,
1587										jtitem->inner_join_rels,
1588										j->jointype,
1589										j->rtindex,
1590										my_quals);
1591			jtitem->sjinfo = sjinfo;
1592			if (j->jointype == JOIN_SEMI)
1593				ojscope = NULL;
1594			else
1595				ojscope = bms_union(sjinfo->min_lefthand,
1596									sjinfo->min_righthand);
1597		}
1598		else
1599		{
1600			sjinfo = NULL;
1601			ojscope = NULL;
1602		}
1603
1604		/*
1605		 * If it's a left join with a join clause that is strict for the LHS,
1606		 * then we need to postpone handling of any non-degenerate join
1607		 * clauses, in case the join is able to commute with another left join
1608		 * per identity 3.  (Degenerate clauses need not be postponed, since
1609		 * they will drop down below this join anyway.)
1610		 */
1611		if (j->jointype == JOIN_LEFT && sjinfo->lhs_strict)
1612		{
1613			postponed_oj_qual_list = &jtitem->oj_joinclauses;
1614
1615			/*
1616			 * Add back any commutable lower OJ relids that were removed from
1617			 * min_lefthand or min_righthand, else the ojscope cross-check in
1618			 * distribute_qual_to_rels will complain.  Since we are postponing
1619			 * processing of non-degenerate clauses, this addition doesn't
1620			 * affect anything except that cross-check.  Real clause
1621			 * positioning decisions will be made later, when we revisit the
1622			 * postponed clauses.
1623			 */
1624			ojscope = bms_add_members(ojscope, sjinfo->commute_below_l);
1625			ojscope = bms_add_members(ojscope, sjinfo->commute_below_r);
1626		}
1627		else
1628			postponed_oj_qual_list = NULL;
1629
1630		/* Process the JOIN's qual clauses */
1631		distribute_quals_to_rels(root, my_quals,
1632								 jtitem,
1633								 sjinfo,
1634								 root->qual_security_level,
1635								 jtitem->qualscope,
1636								 ojscope, jtitem->nonnullable_rels,
1637								 NULL,	/* incompatible_relids */
1638								 true,	/* allow_equivalence */
1639								 false, false,	/* not clones */
1640								 postponed_oj_qual_list);
1641
1642		/* And add the SpecialJoinInfo to join_info_list */
1643		if (sjinfo)
1644			root->join_info_list = lappend(root->join_info_list, sjinfo);
1645	}
1646	else
1647	{
1648		elog(ERROR, "unrecognized node type: %d",
1649			 (int) nodeTag(jtnode));
1650	}
1651}
1652
1653/*
1654 * process_security_barrier_quals
1655 *	  Transfer security-barrier quals into relation's baserestrictinfo list.
1656 *
1657 * The rewriter put any relevant security-barrier conditions into the RTE's
1658 * securityQuals field, but it's now time to copy them into the rel's
1659 * baserestrictinfo.
1660 *
1661 * In inheritance cases, we only consider quals attached to the parent rel
1662 * here; they will be valid for all children too, so it's okay to consider
1663 * them for purposes like equivalence class creation.  Quals attached to
1664 * individual child rels will be dealt with during path creation.
1665 */
1666static void
1667process_security_barrier_quals(PlannerInfo *root,
1668							   int rti, JoinTreeItem *jtitem)
1669{
1670	RangeTblEntry *rte = root->simple_rte_array[rti];
1671	Index		security_level = 0;
1672	ListCell   *lc;
1673
1674	/*
1675	 * Each element of the securityQuals list has been preprocessed into an
1676	 * implicitly-ANDed list of clauses.  All the clauses in a given sublist
1677	 * should get the same security level, but successive sublists get higher
1678	 * levels.
1679	 */
1680	foreach(lc, rte->securityQuals)
1681	{
1682		List	   *qualset = (List *) lfirst(lc);
1683
1684		/*
1685		 * We cheat to the extent of passing ojscope = qualscope rather than
1686		 * its more logical value of NULL.  The only effect this has is to
1687		 * force a Var-free qual to be evaluated at the rel rather than being
1688		 * pushed up to top of tree, which we don't want.
1689		 */
1690		distribute_quals_to_rels(root, qualset,
1691								 jtitem,
1692								 NULL,
1693								 security_level,
1694								 jtitem->qualscope,
1695								 jtitem->qualscope,
1696								 NULL,
1697								 NULL,
1698								 true,
1699								 false, false,	/* not clones */
1700								 NULL);
1701		security_level++;
1702	}
1703
1704	/* Assert that qual_security_level is higher than anything we just used */
1705	Assert(security_level <= root->qual_security_level);
1706}
1707
1708/*
1709 * mark_rels_nulled_by_join
1710 *	  Fill RelOptInfo.nulling_relids of baserels nulled by this outer join
1711 *
1712 * Inputs:
1713 *	ojrelid: RT index of the join RTE (must not be 0)
1714 *	lower_rels: the base+OJ Relids syntactically below nullable side of join
1715 */
1716static void
1717mark_rels_nulled_by_join(PlannerInfo *root, Index ojrelid,
1718						 Relids lower_rels)
1719{
1720	int			relid = -1;
1721
1722	while ((relid = bms_next_member(lower_rels, relid)) > 0)
1723	{
1724		RelOptInfo *rel = root->simple_rel_array[relid];
1725
1726		/* ignore the RTE_GROUP RTE */
1727		if (relid == root->group_rtindex)
1728			continue;
1729
1730		if (rel == NULL)		/* must be an outer join */
1731		{
1732			Assert(bms_is_member(relid, root->outer_join_rels));
1733			continue;
1734		}
1735		rel->nulling_relids = bms_add_member(rel->nulling_relids, ojrelid);
1736	}
1737}
1738
1739/*
1740 * make_outerjoininfo
1741 *	  Build a SpecialJoinInfo for the current outer join
1742 *
1743 * Inputs:
1744 *	left_rels: the base+OJ Relids syntactically on outer side of join
1745 *	right_rels: the base+OJ Relids syntactically on inner side of join
1746 *	inner_join_rels: base+OJ Relids participating in inner joins below this one
1747 *	jointype: what it says (must always be LEFT, FULL, SEMI, or ANTI)
1748 *	ojrelid: RT index of the join RTE (0 for SEMI, which isn't in the RT list)
1749 *	clause: the outer join's join condition (in implicit-AND format)
1750 *
1751 * The node should eventually be appended to root->join_info_list, but we
1752 * do not do that here.
1753 *
1754 * Note: we assume that this function is invoked bottom-up, so that
1755 * root->join_info_list already contains entries for all outer joins that are
1756 * syntactically below this one.
1757 */
1758static SpecialJoinInfo *
1759make_outerjoininfo(PlannerInfo *root,
1760				   Relids left_rels, Relids right_rels,
1761				   Relids inner_join_rels,
1762				   JoinType jointype, Index ojrelid,
1763				   List *clause)
1764{
1765	SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo = makeNode(SpecialJoinInfo);
1766	Relids		clause_relids;
1767	Relids		strict_relids;
1768	Relids		min_lefthand;
1769	Relids		min_righthand;
1770	Relids		commute_below_l;
1771	Relids		commute_below_r;
1772	ListCell   *l;
1773
1774	/*
1775	 * We should not see RIGHT JOIN here because left/right were switched
1776	 * earlier
1777	 */
1778	Assert(jointype != JOIN_INNER);
1779	Assert(jointype != JOIN_RIGHT);
1780
1781	/*
1782	 * Presently the executor cannot support FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE marking of
1783	 * rels appearing on the nullable side of an outer join. (It's somewhat
1784	 * unclear what that would mean, anyway: what should we mark when a result
1785	 * row is generated from no element of the nullable relation?)	So,
1786	 * complain if any nullable rel is FOR [KEY] UPDATE/SHARE.
1787	 *
1788	 * You might be wondering why this test isn't made far upstream in the
1789	 * parser.  It's because the parser hasn't got enough info --- consider
1790	 * FOR UPDATE applied to a view.  Only after rewriting and flattening do
1791	 * we know whether the view contains an outer join.
1792	 *
1793	 * We use the original RowMarkClause list here; the PlanRowMark list would
1794	 * list everything.
1795	 */
1796	foreach(l, root->parse->rowMarks)
1797	{
1798		RowMarkClause *rc = (RowMarkClause *) lfirst(l);
1799
1800		if (bms_is_member(rc->rti, right_rels) ||
1801			(jointype == JOIN_FULL && bms_is_member(rc->rti, left_rels)))
1802			ereport(ERROR,
1803					(errcode(ERRCODE_FEATURE_NOT_SUPPORTED),
1804			/*------
1805			 translator: %s is a SQL row locking clause such as FOR UPDATE */
1806					 errmsg("%s cannot be applied to the nullable side of an outer join",
1807							LCS_asString(rc->strength))));
1808	}
1809
1810	sjinfo->syn_lefthand = left_rels;
1811	sjinfo->syn_righthand = right_rels;
1812	sjinfo->jointype = jointype;
1813	sjinfo->ojrelid = ojrelid;
1814	/* these fields may get added to later: */
1815	sjinfo->commute_above_l = NULL;
1816	sjinfo->commute_above_r = NULL;
1817	sjinfo->commute_below_l = NULL;
1818	sjinfo->commute_below_r = NULL;
1819
1820	compute_semijoin_info(root, sjinfo, clause);
1821
1822	/* If it's a full join, no need to be very smart */
1823	if (jointype == JOIN_FULL)
1824	{
1825		sjinfo->min_lefthand = bms_copy(left_rels);
1826		sjinfo->min_righthand = bms_copy(right_rels);
1827		sjinfo->lhs_strict = false; /* don't care about this */
1828		return sjinfo;
1829	}
1830
1831	/*
1832	 * Retrieve all relids mentioned within the join clause.
1833	 */
1834	clause_relids = pull_varnos(root, (Node *) clause);
1835
1836	/*
1837	 * For which relids is the clause strict, ie, it cannot succeed if the
1838	 * rel's columns are all NULL?
1839	 */
1840	strict_relids = find_nonnullable_rels((Node *) clause);
1841
1842	/* Remember whether the clause is strict for any LHS relations */
1843	sjinfo->lhs_strict = bms_overlap(strict_relids, left_rels);
1844
1845	/*
1846	 * Required LHS always includes the LHS rels mentioned in the clause. We
1847	 * may have to add more rels based on lower outer joins; see below.
1848	 */
1849	min_lefthand = bms_intersect(clause_relids, left_rels);
1850
1851	/*
1852	 * Similarly for required RHS.  But here, we must also include any lower
1853	 * inner joins, to ensure we don't try to commute with any of them.
1854	 */
1855	min_righthand = bms_int_members(bms_union(clause_relids, inner_join_rels),
1856									right_rels);
1857
1858	/*
1859	 * Now check previous outer joins for ordering restrictions.
1860	 *
1861	 * commute_below_l and commute_below_r accumulate the relids of lower
1862	 * outer joins that we think this one can commute with.  These decisions
1863	 * are just tentative within this loop, since we might find an
1864	 * intermediate outer join that prevents commutation.  Surviving relids
1865	 * will get merged into the SpecialJoinInfo structs afterwards.
1866	 */
1867	commute_below_l = commute_below_r = NULL;
1868	foreach(l, root->join_info_list)
1869	{
1870		SpecialJoinInfo *otherinfo = (SpecialJoinInfo *) lfirst(l);
1871		bool		have_unsafe_phvs;
1872
1873		/*
1874		 * A full join is an optimization barrier: we can't associate into or
1875		 * out of it.  Hence, if it overlaps either LHS or RHS of the current
1876		 * rel, expand that side's min relset to cover the whole full join.
1877		 */
1878		if (otherinfo->jointype == JOIN_FULL)
1879		{
1880			Assert(otherinfo->ojrelid != 0);
1881			if (bms_overlap(left_rels, otherinfo->syn_lefthand) ||
1882				bms_overlap(left_rels, otherinfo->syn_righthand))
1883			{
1884				min_lefthand = bms_add_members(min_lefthand,
1885											   otherinfo->syn_lefthand);
1886				min_lefthand = bms_add_members(min_lefthand,
1887											   otherinfo->syn_righthand);
1888				min_lefthand = bms_add_member(min_lefthand,
1889											  otherinfo->ojrelid);
1890			}
1891			if (bms_overlap(right_rels, otherinfo->syn_lefthand) ||
1892				bms_overlap(right_rels, otherinfo->syn_righthand))
1893			{
1894				min_righthand = bms_add_members(min_righthand,
1895												otherinfo->syn_lefthand);
1896				min_righthand = bms_add_members(min_righthand,
1897												otherinfo->syn_righthand);
1898				min_righthand = bms_add_member(min_righthand,
1899											   otherinfo->ojrelid);
1900			}
1901			/* Needn't do anything else with the full join */
1902			continue;
1903		}
1904
1905		/*
1906		 * If our join condition contains any PlaceHolderVars that need to be
1907		 * evaluated above the lower OJ, then we can't commute with it.
1908		 */
1909		if (otherinfo->ojrelid != 0)
1910			have_unsafe_phvs =
1911				contain_placeholder_references_to(root,
1912												  (Node *) clause,
1913												  otherinfo->ojrelid);
1914		else
1915			have_unsafe_phvs = false;
1916
1917		/*
1918		 * For a lower OJ in our LHS, if our join condition uses the lower
1919		 * join's RHS and is not strict for that rel, we must preserve the
1920		 * ordering of the two OJs, so add lower OJ's full syntactic relset to
1921		 * min_lefthand.  (We must use its full syntactic relset, not just its
1922		 * min_lefthand + min_righthand.  This is because there might be other
1923		 * OJs below this one that this one can commute with, but we cannot
1924		 * commute with them if we don't with this one.)  Also, if we have
1925		 * unsafe PHVs or the current join is a semijoin or antijoin, we must
1926		 * preserve ordering regardless of strictness.
1927		 *
1928		 * Note: I believe we have to insist on being strict for at least one
1929		 * rel in the lower OJ's min_righthand, not its whole syn_righthand.
1930		 *
1931		 * When we don't need to preserve ordering, check to see if outer join
1932		 * identity 3 applies, and if so, remove the lower OJ's ojrelid from
1933		 * our min_lefthand so that commutation is allowed.
1934		 */
1935		if (bms_overlap(left_rels, otherinfo->syn_righthand))
1936		{
1937			if (bms_overlap(clause_relids, otherinfo->syn_righthand) &&
1938				(have_unsafe_phvs ||
1939				 jointype == JOIN_SEMI || jointype == JOIN_ANTI ||
1940				 !bms_overlap(strict_relids, otherinfo->min_righthand)))
1941			{
1942				/* Preserve ordering */
1943				min_lefthand = bms_add_members(min_lefthand,
1944											   otherinfo->syn_lefthand);
1945				min_lefthand = bms_add_members(min_lefthand,
1946											   otherinfo->syn_righthand);
1947				if (otherinfo->ojrelid != 0)
1948					min_lefthand = bms_add_member(min_lefthand,
1949												  otherinfo->ojrelid);
1950			}
1951			else if (jointype == JOIN_LEFT &&
1952					 otherinfo->jointype == JOIN_LEFT &&
1953					 bms_overlap(strict_relids, otherinfo->min_righthand) &&
1954					 !bms_overlap(clause_relids, otherinfo->syn_lefthand))
1955			{
1956				/* Identity 3 applies, so remove the ordering restriction */
1957				min_lefthand = bms_del_member(min_lefthand, otherinfo->ojrelid);
1958				/* Record the (still tentative) commutability relationship */
1959				commute_below_l =
1960					bms_add_member(commute_below_l, otherinfo->ojrelid);
1961			}
1962		}
1963
1964		/*
1965		 * For a lower OJ in our RHS, if our join condition does not use the
1966		 * lower join's RHS and the lower OJ's join condition is strict, we
1967		 * can interchange the ordering of the two OJs; otherwise we must add
1968		 * the lower OJ's full syntactic relset to min_righthand.
1969		 *
1970		 * Also, if our join condition does not use the lower join's LHS
1971		 * either, force the ordering to be preserved.  Otherwise we can end
1972		 * up with SpecialJoinInfos with identical min_righthands, which can
1973		 * confuse join_is_legal (see discussion in backend/optimizer/README).
1974		 *
1975		 * Also, we must preserve ordering anyway if we have unsafe PHVs, or
1976		 * if either this join or the lower OJ is a semijoin or antijoin.
1977		 *
1978		 * When we don't need to preserve ordering, check to see if outer join
1979		 * identity 3 applies, and if so, remove the lower OJ's ojrelid from
1980		 * our min_righthand so that commutation is allowed.
1981		 */
1982		if (bms_overlap(right_rels, otherinfo->syn_righthand))
1983		{
1984			if (bms_overlap(clause_relids, otherinfo->syn_righthand) ||
1985				!bms_overlap(clause_relids, otherinfo->min_lefthand) ||
1986				have_unsafe_phvs ||
1987				jointype == JOIN_SEMI ||
1988				jointype == JOIN_ANTI ||
1989				otherinfo->jointype == JOIN_SEMI ||
1990				otherinfo->jointype == JOIN_ANTI ||
1991				!otherinfo->lhs_strict)
1992			{
1993				/* Preserve ordering */
1994				min_righthand = bms_add_members(min_righthand,
1995												otherinfo->syn_lefthand);
1996				min_righthand = bms_add_members(min_righthand,
1997												otherinfo->syn_righthand);
1998				if (otherinfo->ojrelid != 0)
1999					min_righthand = bms_add_member(min_righthand,
2000												   otherinfo->ojrelid);
2001			}
2002			else if (jointype == JOIN_LEFT &&
2003					 otherinfo->jointype == JOIN_LEFT &&
2004					 otherinfo->lhs_strict)
2005			{
2006				/* Identity 3 applies, so remove the ordering restriction */
2007				min_righthand = bms_del_member(min_righthand,
2008											   otherinfo->ojrelid);
2009				/* Record the (still tentative) commutability relationship */
2010				commute_below_r =
2011					bms_add_member(commute_below_r, otherinfo->ojrelid);
2012			}
2013		}
2014	}
2015
2016	/*
2017	 * Examine PlaceHolderVars.  If a PHV is supposed to be evaluated within
2018	 * this join's nullable side, then ensure that min_righthand contains the
2019	 * full eval_at set of the PHV.  This ensures that the PHV actually can be
2020	 * evaluated within the RHS.  Note that this works only because we should
2021	 * already have determined the final eval_at level for any PHV
2022	 * syntactically within this join.
2023	 */
2024	foreach(l, root->placeholder_list)
2025	{
2026		PlaceHolderInfo *phinfo = (PlaceHolderInfo *) lfirst(l);
2027		Relids		ph_syn_level = phinfo->ph_var->phrels;
2028
2029		/* Ignore placeholder if it didn't syntactically come from RHS */
2030		if (!bms_is_subset(ph_syn_level, right_rels))
2031			continue;
2032
2033		/* Else, prevent join from being formed before we eval the PHV */
2034		min_righthand = bms_add_members(min_righthand, phinfo->ph_eval_at);
2035	}
2036
2037	/*
2038	 * If we found nothing to put in min_lefthand, punt and make it the full
2039	 * LHS, to avoid having an empty min_lefthand which will confuse later
2040	 * processing. (We don't try to be smart about such cases, just correct.)
2041	 * Likewise for min_righthand.
2042	 */
2043	if (bms_is_empty(min_lefthand))
2044		min_lefthand = bms_copy(left_rels);
2045	if (bms_is_empty(min_righthand))
2046		min_righthand = bms_copy(right_rels);
2047
2048	/* Now they'd better be nonempty */
2049	Assert(!bms_is_empty(min_lefthand));
2050	Assert(!bms_is_empty(min_righthand));
2051	/* Shouldn't overlap either */
2052	Assert(!bms_overlap(min_lefthand, min_righthand));
2053
2054	sjinfo->min_lefthand = min_lefthand;
2055	sjinfo->min_righthand = min_righthand;
2056
2057	/*
2058	 * Now that we've identified the correct min_lefthand and min_righthand,
2059	 * any commute_below_l or commute_below_r relids that have not gotten
2060	 * added back into those sets (due to intervening outer joins) are indeed
2061	 * commutable with this one.
2062	 *
2063	 * First, delete any subsequently-added-back relids (this is easier than
2064	 * maintaining commute_below_l/r precisely through all the above).
2065	 */
2066	commute_below_l = bms_del_members(commute_below_l, min_lefthand);
2067	commute_below_r = bms_del_members(commute_below_r, min_righthand);
2068
2069	/* Anything left? */
2070	if (commute_below_l || commute_below_r)
2071	{
2072		/* Yup, so we must update the derived data in the SpecialJoinInfos */
2073		sjinfo->commute_below_l = commute_below_l;
2074		sjinfo->commute_below_r = commute_below_r;
2075		foreach(l, root->join_info_list)
2076		{
2077			SpecialJoinInfo *otherinfo = (SpecialJoinInfo *) lfirst(l);
2078
2079			if (bms_is_member(otherinfo->ojrelid, commute_below_l))
2080				otherinfo->commute_above_l =
2081					bms_add_member(otherinfo->commute_above_l, ojrelid);
2082			else if (bms_is_member(otherinfo->ojrelid, commute_below_r))
2083				otherinfo->commute_above_r =
2084					bms_add_member(otherinfo->commute_above_r, ojrelid);
2085		}
2086	}
2087
2088	return sjinfo;
2089}
2090
2091/*
2092 * compute_semijoin_info
2093 *	  Fill semijoin-related fields of a new SpecialJoinInfo
2094 *
2095 * Note: this relies on only the jointype and syn_righthand fields of the
2096 * SpecialJoinInfo; the rest may not be set yet.
2097 */
2098static void
2099compute_semijoin_info(PlannerInfo *root, SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo, List *clause)
2100{
2101	List	   *semi_operators;
2102	List	   *semi_rhs_exprs;
2103	bool		all_btree;
2104	bool		all_hash;
2105	ListCell   *lc;
2106
2107	/* Initialize semijoin-related fields in case we can't unique-ify */
2108	sjinfo->semi_can_btree = false;
2109	sjinfo->semi_can_hash = false;
2110	sjinfo->semi_operators = NIL;
2111	sjinfo->semi_rhs_exprs = NIL;
2112
2113	/* Nothing more to do if it's not a semijoin */
2114	if (sjinfo->jointype != JOIN_SEMI)
2115		return;
2116
2117	/*
2118	 * Look to see whether the semijoin's join quals consist of AND'ed
2119	 * equality operators, with (only) RHS variables on only one side of each
2120	 * one.  If so, we can figure out how to enforce uniqueness for the RHS.
2121	 *
2122	 * Note that the input clause list is the list of quals that are
2123	 * *syntactically* associated with the semijoin, which in practice means
2124	 * the synthesized comparison list for an IN or the WHERE of an EXISTS.
2125	 * Particularly in the latter case, it might contain clauses that aren't
2126	 * *semantically* associated with the join, but refer to just one side or
2127	 * the other.  We can ignore such clauses here, as they will just drop
2128	 * down to be processed within one side or the other.  (It is okay to
2129	 * consider only the syntactically-associated clauses here because for a
2130	 * semijoin, no higher-level quals could refer to the RHS, and so there
2131	 * can be no other quals that are semantically associated with this join.
2132	 * We do things this way because it is useful to have the set of potential
2133	 * unique-ification expressions before we can extract the list of quals
2134	 * that are actually semantically associated with the particular join.)
2135	 *
2136	 * Note that the semi_operators list consists of the joinqual operators
2137	 * themselves (but commuted if needed to put the RHS value on the right).
2138	 * These could be cross-type operators, in which case the operator
2139	 * actually needed for uniqueness is a related single-type operator. We
2140	 * assume here that that operator will be available from the btree or hash
2141	 * opclass when the time comes ... if not, create_unique_plan() will fail.
2142	 */
2143	semi_operators = NIL;
2144	semi_rhs_exprs = NIL;
2145	all_btree = true;
2146	all_hash = enable_hashagg;	/* don't consider hash if not enabled */
2147	foreach(lc, clause)
2148	{
2149		OpExpr	   *op = (OpExpr *) lfirst(lc);
2150		Oid			opno;
2151		Node	   *left_expr;
2152		Node	   *right_expr;
2153		Relids		left_varnos;
2154		Relids		right_varnos;
2155		Relids		all_varnos;
2156		Oid			opinputtype;
2157
2158		/* Is it a binary opclause? */
2159		if (!IsA(op, OpExpr) ||
2160			list_length(op->args) != 2)
2161		{
2162			/* No, but does it reference both sides? */
2163			all_varnos = pull_varnos(root, (Node *) op);
2164			if (!bms_overlap(all_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand) ||
2165				bms_is_subset(all_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand))
2166			{
2167				/*
2168				 * Clause refers to only one rel, so ignore it --- unless it
2169				 * contains volatile functions, in which case we'd better
2170				 * punt.
2171				 */
2172				if (contain_volatile_functions((Node *) op))
2173					return;
2174				continue;
2175			}
2176			/* Non-operator clause referencing both sides, must punt */
2177			return;
2178		}
2179
2180		/* Extract data from binary opclause */
2181		opno = op->opno;
2182		left_expr = linitial(op->args);
2183		right_expr = lsecond(op->args);
2184		left_varnos = pull_varnos(root, left_expr);
2185		right_varnos = pull_varnos(root, right_expr);
2186		all_varnos = bms_union(left_varnos, right_varnos);
2187		opinputtype = exprType(left_expr);
2188
2189		/* Does it reference both sides? */
2190		if (!bms_overlap(all_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand) ||
2191			bms_is_subset(all_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand))
2192		{
2193			/*
2194			 * Clause refers to only one rel, so ignore it --- unless it
2195			 * contains volatile functions, in which case we'd better punt.
2196			 */
2197			if (contain_volatile_functions((Node *) op))
2198				return;
2199			continue;
2200		}
2201
2202		/* check rel membership of arguments */
2203		if (!bms_is_empty(right_varnos) &&
2204			bms_is_subset(right_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand) &&
2205			!bms_overlap(left_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand))
2206		{
2207			/* typical case, right_expr is RHS variable */
2208		}
