2209		else if (!bms_is_empty(left_varnos) &&
2210				 bms_is_subset(left_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand) &&
2211				 !bms_overlap(right_varnos, sjinfo->syn_righthand))
2212		{
2213			/* flipped case, left_expr is RHS variable */
2214			opno = get_commutator(opno);
2215			if (!OidIsValid(opno))
2216				return;
2217			right_expr = left_expr;
2218		}
2219		else
2220		{
2221			/* mixed membership of args, punt */
2222			return;
2223		}
2224
2225		/* all operators must be btree equality or hash equality */
2226		if (all_btree)
2227		{
2228			/* oprcanmerge is considered a hint... */
2229			if (!op_mergejoinable(opno, opinputtype) ||
2230				get_mergejoin_opfamilies(opno) == NIL)
2231				all_btree = false;
2232		}
2233		if (all_hash)
2234		{
2235			/* ... but oprcanhash had better be correct */
2236			if (!op_hashjoinable(opno, opinputtype))
2237				all_hash = false;
2238		}
2239		if (!(all_btree || all_hash))
2240			return;
2241
2242		/* so far so good, keep building lists */
2243		semi_operators = lappend_oid(semi_operators, opno);
2244		semi_rhs_exprs = lappend(semi_rhs_exprs, copyObject(right_expr));
2245	}
2246
2247	/* Punt if we didn't find at least one column to unique-ify */
2248	if (semi_rhs_exprs == NIL)
2249		return;
2250
2251	/*
2252	 * The expressions we'd need to unique-ify mustn't be volatile.
2253	 */
2254	if (contain_volatile_functions((Node *) semi_rhs_exprs))
2255		return;
2256
2257	/*
2258	 * If we get here, we can unique-ify the semijoin's RHS using at least one
2259	 * of sorting and hashing.  Save the information about how to do that.
2260	 */
2261	sjinfo->semi_can_btree = all_btree;
2262	sjinfo->semi_can_hash = all_hash;
2263	sjinfo->semi_operators = semi_operators;
2264	sjinfo->semi_rhs_exprs = semi_rhs_exprs;
2265}
2266
2267/*
2268 * deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals
2269 *	  Adjust LEFT JOIN quals to be suitable for commuted-left-join cases,
2270 *	  then push them into the joinqual lists and EquivalenceClass structures.
2271 *
2272 * This runs immediately after we've completed the deconstruct_distribute scan.
2273 * jtitems contains all the JoinTreeItems (in depth-first order), and jtitem
2274 * is one that has postponed oj_joinclauses to deal with.
2275 */
2276static void
2277deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals(PlannerInfo *root,
2278								List *jtitems,
2279								JoinTreeItem *jtitem)
2280{
2281	SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo = jtitem->sjinfo;
2282	Relids		qualscope,
2283				ojscope,
2284				nonnullable_rels;
2285
2286	/* Recompute syntactic and semantic scopes of this left join */
2287	qualscope = bms_union(sjinfo->syn_lefthand, sjinfo->syn_righthand);
2288	qualscope = bms_add_member(qualscope, sjinfo->ojrelid);
2289	ojscope = bms_union(sjinfo->min_lefthand, sjinfo->min_righthand);
2290	nonnullable_rels = sjinfo->syn_lefthand;
2291
2292	/*
2293	 * If this join can commute with any other ones per outer-join identity 3,
2294	 * and it is the one providing the join clause with flexible semantics,
2295	 * then we have to generate variants of the join clause with different
2296	 * nullingrels labeling.  Otherwise, just push out the postponed clause
2297	 * as-is.
2298	 */
2299	Assert(sjinfo->lhs_strict); /* else we shouldn't be here */
2300	if (sjinfo->commute_above_r || sjinfo->commute_below_l)
2301	{
2302		Relids		joins_above;
2303		Relids		joins_below;
2304		Relids		incompatible_joins;
2305		Relids		joins_so_far;
2306		List	   *quals;
2307		int			save_last_rinfo_serial;
2308		ListCell   *lc;
2309
2310		/* Identify the outer joins this one commutes with */
2311		joins_above = sjinfo->commute_above_r;
2312		joins_below = sjinfo->commute_below_l;
2313
2314		/*
2315		 * Generate qual variants with different sets of nullingrels bits.
2316		 *
2317		 * We only need bit-sets that correspond to the successively less
2318		 * deeply syntactically-nested subsets of this join and its
2319		 * commutators.  That's true first because obviously only those forms
2320		 * of the Vars and PHVs could appear elsewhere in the query, and
2321		 * second because the outer join identities do not provide a way to
2322		 * re-order such joins in a way that would require different marking.
2323		 * (That is, while the current join may commute with several others,
2324		 * none of those others can commute with each other.)  To visit the
2325		 * interesting joins in syntactic nesting order, we rely on the
2326		 * jtitems list to be ordered that way.
2327		 *
2328		 * We first strip out all the nullingrels bits corresponding to
2329		 * commuting joins below this one, and then successively put them back
2330		 * as we crawl up the join stack.
2331		 */
2332		quals = jtitem->oj_joinclauses;
2333		if (!bms_is_empty(joins_below))
2334			quals = (List *) remove_nulling_relids((Node *) quals,
2335												   joins_below,
2336												   NULL);
2337
2338		/*
2339		 * We'll need to mark the lower versions of the quals as not safe to
2340		 * apply above not-yet-processed joins of the stack.  This prevents
2341		 * possibly applying a cloned qual at the wrong join level.
2342		 */
2343		incompatible_joins = bms_union(joins_below, joins_above);
2344		incompatible_joins = bms_add_member(incompatible_joins,
2345											sjinfo->ojrelid);
2346
2347		/*
2348		 * Each time we produce RestrictInfo(s) from these quals, reset the
2349		 * last_rinfo_serial counter, so that the RestrictInfos for the "same"
2350		 * qual condition get identical serial numbers.  (This relies on the
2351		 * fact that we're not changing the qual list in any way that'd affect
2352		 * the number of RestrictInfos built from it.) This'll allow us to
2353		 * detect duplicative qual usage later.
2354		 */
2355		save_last_rinfo_serial = root->last_rinfo_serial;
2356
2357		joins_so_far = NULL;
2358		foreach(lc, jtitems)
2359		{
2360			JoinTreeItem *otherjtitem = (JoinTreeItem *) lfirst(lc);
2361			SpecialJoinInfo *othersj = otherjtitem->sjinfo;
2362			bool		below_sjinfo = false;
2363			bool		above_sjinfo = false;
2364			Relids		this_qualscope;
2365			Relids		this_ojscope;
2366			bool		allow_equivalence,
2367						has_clone,
2368						is_clone;
2369
2370			if (othersj == NULL)
2371				continue;		/* not an outer-join item, ignore */
2372
2373			if (bms_is_member(othersj->ojrelid, joins_below))
2374			{
2375				/* othersj commutes with sjinfo from below left */
2376				below_sjinfo = true;
2377			}
2378			else if (othersj == sjinfo)
2379			{
2380				/* found our join in syntactic order */
2381				Assert(bms_equal(joins_so_far, joins_below));
2382			}
2383			else if (bms_is_member(othersj->ojrelid, joins_above))
2384			{
2385				/* othersj commutes with sjinfo from above */
2386				above_sjinfo = true;
2387			}
2388			else
2389			{
2390				/* othersj is not relevant, ignore */
2391				continue;
2392			}
2393
2394			/* Reset serial counter for this version of the quals */
2395			root->last_rinfo_serial = save_last_rinfo_serial;
2396
2397			/*
2398			 * When we are looking at joins above sjinfo, we are envisioning
2399			 * pushing sjinfo to above othersj, so add othersj's nulling bit
2400			 * before distributing the quals.  We should add it to Vars coming
2401			 * from the current join's LHS: we want to transform the second
2402			 * form of OJ identity 3 to the first form, in which Vars of
2403			 * relation B will appear nulled by the syntactically-upper OJ
2404			 * within the Pbc clause, but those of relation C will not.  (In
2405			 * the notation used by optimizer/README, we're converting a qual
2406			 * of the form Pbc to Pb*c.)  Of course, we must also remove that
2407			 * bit from the incompatible_joins value, else we'll make a qual
2408			 * that can't be placed anywhere.
2409			 */
2410			if (above_sjinfo)
2411			{
2412				quals = (List *)
2413					add_nulling_relids((Node *) quals,
2414									   sjinfo->syn_lefthand,
2415									   bms_make_singleton(othersj->ojrelid));
2416				incompatible_joins = bms_del_member(incompatible_joins,
2417													othersj->ojrelid);
2418			}
2419
2420			/* Compute qualscope and ojscope for this join level */
2421			this_qualscope = bms_union(qualscope, joins_so_far);
2422			this_ojscope = bms_union(ojscope, joins_so_far);
2423			if (above_sjinfo)
2424			{
2425				/* othersj is not yet in joins_so_far, but we need it */
2426				this_qualscope = bms_add_member(this_qualscope,
2427												othersj->ojrelid);
2428				this_ojscope = bms_add_member(this_ojscope,
2429											  othersj->ojrelid);
2430				/* sjinfo is in joins_so_far, and we don't want it */
2431				this_ojscope = bms_del_member(this_ojscope,
2432											  sjinfo->ojrelid);
2433			}
2434
2435			/*
2436			 * We generate EquivalenceClasses only from the first form of the
2437			 * quals, with the fewest nullingrels bits set.  An EC made from
2438			 * this version of the quals can be useful below the outer-join
2439			 * nest, whereas versions with some nullingrels bits set would not
2440			 * be.  We cannot generate ECs from more than one version, or
2441			 * we'll make nonsensical conclusions that Vars with nullingrels
2442			 * bits set are equal to their versions without.  Fortunately,
2443			 * such ECs wouldn't be very useful anyway, because they'd equate
2444			 * values not observable outside the join nest.  (See
2445			 * optimizer/README.)
2446			 *
2447			 * The first form of the quals is also the only one marked as
2448			 * has_clone rather than is_clone.
2449			 */
2450			allow_equivalence = (joins_so_far == NULL);
2451			has_clone = allow_equivalence;
2452			is_clone = !has_clone;
2453
2454			distribute_quals_to_rels(root, quals,
2455									 otherjtitem,
2456									 sjinfo,
2457									 root->qual_security_level,
2458									 this_qualscope,
2459									 this_ojscope, nonnullable_rels,
2460									 bms_copy(incompatible_joins),
2461									 allow_equivalence,
2462									 has_clone,
2463									 is_clone,
2464									 NULL); /* no more postponement */
2465
2466			/*
2467			 * Adjust qual nulling bits for next level up, if needed.  We
2468			 * don't want to put sjinfo's own bit in at all, and if we're
2469			 * above sjinfo then we did it already.  Here, we should mark all
2470			 * Vars coming from the lower join's RHS.  (Again, we are
2471			 * converting a qual of the form Pbc to Pb*c, but now we are
2472			 * putting back bits that were there in the parser output and were
2473			 * temporarily stripped above.)  Update incompatible_joins too.
2474			 */
2475			if (below_sjinfo)
2476			{
2477				quals = (List *)
2478					add_nulling_relids((Node *) quals,
2479									   othersj->syn_righthand,
2480									   bms_make_singleton(othersj->ojrelid));
2481				incompatible_joins = bms_del_member(incompatible_joins,
2482													othersj->ojrelid);
2483			}
2484
2485			/* ... and track joins processed so far */
2486			joins_so_far = bms_add_member(joins_so_far, othersj->ojrelid);
2487		}
2488	}
2489	else
2490	{
2491		/* No commutation possible, just process the postponed clauses */
2492		distribute_quals_to_rels(root, jtitem->oj_joinclauses,
2493								 jtitem,
2494								 sjinfo,
2495								 root->qual_security_level,
2496								 qualscope,
2497								 ojscope, nonnullable_rels,
2498								 NULL,	/* incompatible_relids */
2499								 true,	/* allow_equivalence */
2500								 false, false,	/* not clones */
2501								 NULL); /* no more postponement */
2502	}
2503}
2504
2505
2506/*****************************************************************************
2507 *
2508 *	  QUALIFICATIONS
2509 *
2510 *****************************************************************************/
2511
2512/*
2513 * distribute_quals_to_rels
2514 *	  Convenience routine to apply distribute_qual_to_rels to each element
2515 *	  of an AND'ed list of clauses.
2516 */
2517static void
2518distribute_quals_to_rels(PlannerInfo *root, List *clauses,
2519						 JoinTreeItem *jtitem,
2520						 SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
2521						 Index security_level,
2522						 Relids qualscope,
2523						 Relids ojscope,
2524						 Relids outerjoin_nonnullable,
2525						 Relids incompatible_relids,
2526						 bool allow_equivalence,
2527						 bool has_clone,
2528						 bool is_clone,
2529						 List **postponed_oj_qual_list)
2530{
2531	ListCell   *lc;
2532
2533	foreach(lc, clauses)
2534	{
2535		Node	   *clause = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
2536
2537		distribute_qual_to_rels(root, clause,
2538								jtitem,
2539								sjinfo,
2540								security_level,
2541								qualscope,
2542								ojscope,
2543								outerjoin_nonnullable,
2544								incompatible_relids,
2545								allow_equivalence,
2546								has_clone,
2547								is_clone,
2548								postponed_oj_qual_list);
2549	}
2550}
2551
2552/*
2553 * distribute_qual_to_rels
2554 *	  Add clause information to either the baserestrictinfo or joininfo list
2555 *	  (depending on whether the clause is a join) of each base relation
2556 *	  mentioned in the clause.  A RestrictInfo node is created and added to
2557 *	  the appropriate list for each rel.  Alternatively, if the clause uses a
2558 *	  mergejoinable operator, enter its left- and right-side expressions into
2559 *	  the query's EquivalenceClasses.
2560 *
2561 * In some cases, quals will be added to parent jtitems' lateral_clauses
2562 * or to postponed_oj_qual_list instead of being processed right away.
2563 * These will be dealt with in later calls of deconstruct_distribute.
2564 *
2565 * 'clause': the qual clause to be distributed
2566 * 'jtitem': the JoinTreeItem for the containing jointree node
2567 * 'sjinfo': join's SpecialJoinInfo (NULL for an inner join or WHERE clause)
2568 * 'security_level': security_level to assign to the qual
2569 * 'qualscope': set of base+OJ rels the qual's syntactic scope covers
2570 * 'ojscope': NULL if not an outer-join qual, else the minimum set of base+OJ
2571 *		rels needed to form this join
2572 * 'outerjoin_nonnullable': NULL if not an outer-join qual, else the set of
2573 *		base+OJ rels appearing on the outer (nonnullable) side of the join
2574 *		(for FULL JOIN this includes both sides of the join, and must in fact
2575 *		equal qualscope)
2576 * 'incompatible_relids': the set of outer-join relid(s) that must not be
2577 *		computed below this qual.  We only bother to compute this for
2578 *		"clone" quals, otherwise it can be left NULL.
2579 * 'allow_equivalence': true if it's okay to convert clause into an
2580 *		EquivalenceClass
2581 * 'has_clone': has_clone property to assign to the qual
2582 * 'is_clone': is_clone property to assign to the qual
2583 * 'postponed_oj_qual_list': if not NULL, non-degenerate outer join clauses
2584 *		should be added to this list instead of being processed (list entries
2585 *		are just the bare clauses)
2586 *
2587 * 'qualscope' identifies what level of JOIN the qual came from syntactically.
2588 * 'ojscope' is needed if we decide to force the qual up to the outer-join
2589 * level, which will be ojscope not necessarily qualscope.
2590 *
2591 * At the time this is called, root->join_info_list must contain entries for
2592 * at least those special joins that are syntactically below this qual.
2593 * (We now need that only for detection of redundant IS NULL quals.)
2594 */
2595static void
2596distribute_qual_to_rels(PlannerInfo *root, Node *clause,
2597						JoinTreeItem *jtitem,
2598						SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo,
2599						Index security_level,
2600						Relids qualscope,
2601						Relids ojscope,
2602						Relids outerjoin_nonnullable,
2603						Relids incompatible_relids,
2604						bool allow_equivalence,
2605						bool has_clone,
2606						bool is_clone,
2607						List **postponed_oj_qual_list)
2608{
2609	Relids		relids;
2610	bool		is_pushed_down;
2611	bool		pseudoconstant = false;
2612	bool		maybe_equivalence;
2613	bool		maybe_outer_join;
2614	RestrictInfo *restrictinfo;
2615
2616	/*
2617	 * Retrieve all relids mentioned within the clause.
2618	 */
2619	relids = pull_varnos(root, clause);
2620
2621	/*
2622	 * In ordinary SQL, a WHERE or JOIN/ON clause can't reference any rels
2623	 * that aren't within its syntactic scope; however, if we pulled up a
2624	 * LATERAL subquery then we might find such references in quals that have
2625	 * been pulled up.  We need to treat such quals as belonging to the join
2626	 * level that includes every rel they reference.  Although we could make
2627	 * pull_up_subqueries() place such quals correctly to begin with, it's
2628	 * easier to handle it here.  When we find a clause that contains Vars
2629	 * outside its syntactic scope, locate the nearest parent join level that
2630	 * includes all the required rels and add the clause to that level's
2631	 * lateral_clauses list.  We'll process it when we reach that join level.
2632	 */
2633	if (!bms_is_subset(relids, qualscope))
2634	{
2635		JoinTreeItem *pitem;
2636
2637		Assert(root->hasLateralRTEs);	/* shouldn't happen otherwise */
2638		Assert(sjinfo == NULL); /* mustn't postpone past outer join */
2639		for (pitem = jtitem->jti_parent; pitem; pitem = pitem->jti_parent)
2640		{
2641			if (bms_is_subset(relids, pitem->qualscope))
2642			{
2643				pitem->lateral_clauses = lappend(pitem->lateral_clauses,
2644												 clause);
2645				return;
2646			}
2647
2648			/*
2649			 * We should not be postponing any quals past an outer join.  If
2650			 * this Assert fires, pull_up_subqueries() messed up.
2651			 */
2652			Assert(pitem->sjinfo == NULL);
2653		}
2654		elog(ERROR, "failed to postpone qual containing lateral reference");
2655	}
2656
2657	/*
2658	 * If it's an outer-join clause, also check that relids is a subset of
2659	 * ojscope.  (This should not fail if the syntactic scope check passed.)
2660	 */
2661	if (ojscope && !bms_is_subset(relids, ojscope))
2662		elog(ERROR, "JOIN qualification cannot refer to other relations");
2663
2664	/*
2665	 * If the clause is variable-free, our normal heuristic for pushing it
2666	 * down to just the mentioned rels doesn't work, because there are none.
2667	 *
2668	 * If the clause is an outer-join clause, we must force it to the OJ's
2669	 * semantic level to preserve semantics.
2670	 *
2671	 * Otherwise, when the clause contains volatile functions, we force it to
2672	 * be evaluated at its original syntactic level.  This preserves the
2673	 * expected semantics.
2674	 *
2675	 * When the clause contains no volatile functions either, it is actually a
2676	 * pseudoconstant clause that will not change value during any one
2677	 * execution of the plan, and hence can be used as a one-time qual in a
2678	 * gating Result plan node.  We put such a clause into the regular
2679	 * RestrictInfo lists for the moment, but eventually createplan.c will
2680	 * pull it out and make a gating Result node immediately above whatever
2681	 * plan node the pseudoconstant clause is assigned to.  It's usually best
2682	 * to put a gating node as high in the plan tree as possible.
2683	 */
2684	if (bms_is_empty(relids))
2685	{
2686		if (ojscope)
2687		{
2688			/* clause is attached to outer join, eval it there */
2689			relids = bms_copy(ojscope);
2690			/* mustn't use as gating qual, so don't mark pseudoconstant */
2691		}
2692		else if (contain_volatile_functions(clause))
2693		{
2694			/* eval at original syntactic level */
2695			relids = bms_copy(qualscope);
2696			/* again, can't mark pseudoconstant */
2697		}
2698		else
2699		{
2700			/*
2701			 * If we are in the top-level join domain, we can push the qual to
2702			 * the top of the plan tree.  Otherwise, be conservative and eval
2703			 * it at original syntactic level.  (Ideally we'd push it to the
2704			 * top of the current join domain in all cases, but that causes
2705			 * problems if we later rearrange outer-join evaluation order.
2706			 * Pseudoconstant quals below the top level are a pretty odd case,
2707			 * so it's not clear that it's worth working hard on.)
2708			 */
2709			if (jtitem->jdomain == (JoinDomain *) linitial(root->join_domains))
2710				relids = bms_copy(jtitem->jdomain->jd_relids);
2711			else
2712				relids = bms_copy(qualscope);
2713			/* mark as gating qual */
2714			pseudoconstant = true;
2715			/* tell createplan.c to check for gating quals */
2716			root->hasPseudoConstantQuals = true;
2717		}
2718	}
2719
2720	/*----------
2721	 * Check to see if clause application must be delayed by outer-join
2722	 * considerations.
2723	 *
2724	 * A word about is_pushed_down: we mark the qual as "pushed down" if
2725	 * it is (potentially) applicable at a level different from its original
2726	 * syntactic level.  This flag is used to distinguish OUTER JOIN ON quals
2727	 * from other quals pushed down to the same joinrel.  The rules are:
2728	 *		WHERE quals and INNER JOIN quals: is_pushed_down = true.
2729	 *		Non-degenerate OUTER JOIN quals: is_pushed_down = false.
2730	 *		Degenerate OUTER JOIN quals: is_pushed_down = true.
2731	 * A "degenerate" OUTER JOIN qual is one that doesn't mention the
2732	 * non-nullable side, and hence can be pushed down into the nullable side
2733	 * without changing the join result.  It is correct to treat it as a
2734	 * regular filter condition at the level where it is evaluated.
2735	 *
2736	 * Note: it is not immediately obvious that a simple boolean is enough
2737	 * for this: if for some reason we were to attach a degenerate qual to
2738	 * its original join level, it would need to be treated as an outer join
2739	 * qual there.  However, this cannot happen, because all the rels the
2740	 * clause mentions must be in the outer join's min_righthand, therefore
2741	 * the join it needs must be formed before the outer join; and we always
2742	 * attach quals to the lowest level where they can be evaluated.  But
2743	 * if we were ever to re-introduce a mechanism for delaying evaluation
2744	 * of "expensive" quals, this area would need work.
2745	 *
2746	 * Note: generally, use of is_pushed_down has to go through the macro
2747	 * RINFO_IS_PUSHED_DOWN, because that flag alone is not always sufficient
2748	 * to tell whether a clause must be treated as pushed-down in context.
2749	 * This seems like another reason why it should perhaps be rethought.
2750	 *----------
2751	 */
2752	if (bms_overlap(relids, outerjoin_nonnullable))
2753	{
2754		/*
2755		 * The qual is attached to an outer join and mentions (some of the)
2756		 * rels on the nonnullable side, so it's not degenerate.  If the
2757		 * caller wants to postpone handling such clauses, just add it to
2758		 * postponed_oj_qual_list and return.  (The work we've done up to here
2759		 * will have to be redone later, but there's not much of it.)
2760		 */
2761		if (postponed_oj_qual_list != NULL)
2762		{
2763			*postponed_oj_qual_list = lappend(*postponed_oj_qual_list, clause);
2764			return;
2765		}
2766
2767		/*
2768		 * We can't use such a clause to deduce equivalence (the left and
2769		 * right sides might be unequal above the join because one of them has
2770		 * gone to NULL) ... but we might be able to use it for more limited
2771		 * deductions, if it is mergejoinable.  So consider adding it to the
2772		 * lists of set-aside outer-join clauses.
2773		 */
2774		is_pushed_down = false;
2775		maybe_equivalence = false;
2776		maybe_outer_join = true;
2777
2778		/*
2779		 * Now force the qual to be evaluated exactly at the level of joining
2780		 * corresponding to the outer join.  We cannot let it get pushed down
2781		 * into the nonnullable side, since then we'd produce no output rows,
2782		 * rather than the intended single null-extended row, for any
2783		 * nonnullable-side rows failing the qual.
2784		 */
2785		Assert(ojscope);
2786		relids = ojscope;
2787		Assert(!pseudoconstant);
2788	}
2789	else
2790	{
2791		/*
2792		 * Normal qual clause or degenerate outer-join clause.  Either way, we
2793		 * can mark it as pushed-down.
2794		 */
2795		is_pushed_down = true;
2796
2797		/*
2798		 * It's possible that this is an IS NULL clause that's redundant with
2799		 * a lower antijoin; if so we can just discard it.  We need not test
2800		 * in any of the other cases, because this will only be possible for
2801		 * pushed-down clauses.
2802		 */
2803		if (check_redundant_nullability_qual(root, clause))
2804			return;
2805
2806		/* Feed qual to the equivalence machinery, if allowed by caller */
2807		maybe_equivalence = allow_equivalence;
2808
2809		/*
2810		 * Since it doesn't mention the LHS, it's certainly not useful as a
2811		 * set-aside OJ clause, even if it's in an OJ.
2812		 */
2813		maybe_outer_join = false;
2814	}
2815
2816	/*
2817	 * Build the RestrictInfo node itself.
2818	 */
2819	restrictinfo = make_restrictinfo(root,
2820									 (Expr *) clause,
2821									 is_pushed_down,
2822									 has_clone,
2823									 is_clone,
2824									 pseudoconstant,
2825									 security_level,
2826									 relids,
2827									 incompatible_relids,
2828									 outerjoin_nonnullable);
2829
2830	/*
2831	 * If it's a join clause, add vars used in the clause to targetlists of
2832	 * their relations, so that they will be emitted by the plan nodes that
2833	 * scan those relations (else they won't be available at the join node!).
2834	 *
2835	 * Normally we mark the vars as needed at the join identified by "relids".
2836	 * However, if this is a clone clause then ignore the outer-join relids in
2837	 * that set.  Otherwise, vars appearing in a cloned clause would end up
2838	 * marked as having to propagate to the highest one of the commuting
2839	 * joins, which would often be an overestimate.  For such clauses, correct
2840	 * var propagation is ensured by making ojscope include input rels from
2841	 * both sides of the join.
2842	 *
2843	 * See also rebuild_joinclause_attr_needed, which has to partially repeat
2844	 * this work after removal of an outer join.
2845	 *
2846	 * Note: if the clause gets absorbed into an EquivalenceClass then this
2847	 * may be unnecessary, but for now we have to do it to cover the case
2848	 * where the EC becomes ec_broken and we end up reinserting the original
2849	 * clauses into the plan.
2850	 */
2851	if (bms_membership(relids) == BMS_MULTIPLE)
2852	{
2853		List	   *vars = pull_var_clause(clause,
2854										   PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
2855										   PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
2856										   PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
2857		Relids		where_needed;
2858
2859		if (is_clone)
2860			where_needed = bms_intersect(relids, root->all_baserels);
2861		else
2862			where_needed = relids;
2863		add_vars_to_targetlist(root, vars, where_needed);
2864		list_free(vars);
2865	}
2866
2867	/*
2868	 * We check "mergejoinability" of every clause, not only join clauses,
2869	 * because we want to know about equivalences between vars of the same
2870	 * relation, or between vars and consts.
2871	 */
2872	check_mergejoinable(restrictinfo);
2873
2874	/*
2875	 * If it is a true equivalence clause, send it to the EquivalenceClass
2876	 * machinery.  We do *not* attach it directly to any restriction or join
2877	 * lists.  The EC code will propagate it to the appropriate places later.
2878	 *
2879	 * If the clause has a mergejoinable operator, yet isn't an equivalence
2880	 * because it is an outer-join clause, the EC code may still be able to do
2881	 * something with it.  We add it to appropriate lists for further
2882	 * consideration later.  Specifically:
2883	 *
2884	 * If it is a left or right outer-join qualification that relates the two
2885	 * sides of the outer join (no funny business like leftvar1 = leftvar2 +
2886	 * rightvar), we add it to root->left_join_clauses or
2887	 * root->right_join_clauses according to which side the nonnullable
2888	 * variable appears on.
2889	 *
2890	 * If it is a full outer-join qualification, we add it to
2891	 * root->full_join_clauses.  (Ideally we'd discard cases that aren't
2892	 * leftvar = rightvar, as we do for left/right joins, but this routine
2893	 * doesn't have the info needed to do that; and the current usage of the
2894	 * full_join_clauses list doesn't require that, so it's not currently
2895	 * worth complicating this routine's API to make it possible.)
2896	 *
2897	 * If none of the above hold, pass it off to
2898	 * distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels().
2899	 *
2900	 * In all cases, it's important to initialize the left_ec and right_ec
2901	 * fields of a mergejoinable clause, so that all possibly mergejoinable
2902	 * expressions have representations in EquivalenceClasses.  If
2903	 * process_equivalence is successful, it will take care of that;
2904	 * otherwise, we have to call initialize_mergeclause_eclasses to do it.
2905	 */
2906	if (restrictinfo->mergeopfamilies)
2907	{
2908		if (maybe_equivalence)
2909		{
2910			if (process_equivalence(root, &restrictinfo, jtitem->jdomain))
2911				return;
2912			/* EC rejected it, so set left_ec/right_ec the hard way ... */
2913			if (restrictinfo->mergeopfamilies)	/* EC might have changed this */
2914				initialize_mergeclause_eclasses(root, restrictinfo);
2915			/* ... and fall through to distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels */
2916		}
2917		else if (maybe_outer_join && restrictinfo->can_join)
2918		{
2919			/* we need to set up left_ec/right_ec the hard way */
2920			initialize_mergeclause_eclasses(root, restrictinfo);
2921			/* now see if it should go to any outer-join lists */
2922			Assert(sjinfo != NULL);
2923			if (bms_is_subset(restrictinfo->left_relids,
2924							  outerjoin_nonnullable) &&
2925				!bms_overlap(restrictinfo->right_relids,
2926							 outerjoin_nonnullable))
2927			{
2928				/* we have outervar = innervar */
2929				OuterJoinClauseInfo *ojcinfo = makeNode(OuterJoinClauseInfo);
2930
2931				ojcinfo->rinfo = restrictinfo;
2932				ojcinfo->sjinfo = sjinfo;
2933				root->left_join_clauses = lappend(root->left_join_clauses,
2934												  ojcinfo);
2935				return;
2936			}
2937			if (bms_is_subset(restrictinfo->right_relids,
2938							  outerjoin_nonnullable) &&
2939				!bms_overlap(restrictinfo->left_relids,
2940							 outerjoin_nonnullable))
2941			{
2942				/* we have innervar = outervar */
2943				OuterJoinClauseInfo *ojcinfo = makeNode(OuterJoinClauseInfo);
2944
2945				ojcinfo->rinfo = restrictinfo;
2946				ojcinfo->sjinfo = sjinfo;
2947				root->right_join_clauses = lappend(root->right_join_clauses,
2948												   ojcinfo);
2949				return;
2950			}
2951			if (sjinfo->jointype == JOIN_FULL)
2952			{
2953				/* FULL JOIN (above tests cannot match in this case) */
2954				OuterJoinClauseInfo *ojcinfo = makeNode(OuterJoinClauseInfo);
2955
2956				ojcinfo->rinfo = restrictinfo;
2957				ojcinfo->sjinfo = sjinfo;
2958				root->full_join_clauses = lappend(root->full_join_clauses,
2959												  ojcinfo);
2960				return;
2961			}
2962			/* nope, so fall through to distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels */
2963		}
2964		else
2965		{
2966			/* we still need to set up left_ec/right_ec */
2967			initialize_mergeclause_eclasses(root, restrictinfo);
2968		}
2969	}
2970
2971	/* No EC special case applies, so push it into the clause lists */
2972	distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels(root, restrictinfo);
2973}
2974
2975/*
2976 * check_redundant_nullability_qual
2977 *	  Check to see if the qual is an IS NULL qual that is redundant with
2978 *	  a lower JOIN_ANTI join.
2979 *
2980 * We want to suppress redundant IS NULL quals, not so much to save cycles
2981 * as to avoid generating bogus selectivity estimates for them.  So if
2982 * redundancy is detected here, distribute_qual_to_rels() just throws away
2983 * the qual.
2984 */
2985static bool
2986check_redundant_nullability_qual(PlannerInfo *root, Node *clause)
2987{
2988	Var		   *forced_null_var;
2989	ListCell   *lc;
2990
2991	/* Check for IS NULL, and identify the Var forced to NULL */
2992	forced_null_var = find_forced_null_var(clause);
2993	if (forced_null_var == NULL)
2994		return false;
2995
2996	/*
2997	 * If the Var comes from the nullable side of a lower antijoin, the IS
2998	 * NULL condition is necessarily true.  If it's not nulled by anything,
2999	 * there is no point in searching the join_info_list.  Otherwise, we need
3000	 * to find out whether the nulling rel is an antijoin.
3001	 */
3002	if (forced_null_var->varnullingrels == NULL)
3003		return false;
3004
3005	foreach(lc, root->join_info_list)
3006	{
3007		SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo = (SpecialJoinInfo *) lfirst(lc);
3008
3009		/*
3010		 * This test will not succeed if sjinfo->ojrelid is zero, which is
3011		 * possible for an antijoin that was converted from a semijoin; but in
3012		 * such a case the Var couldn't have come from its nullable side.
3013		 */
3014		if (sjinfo->jointype == JOIN_ANTI && sjinfo->ojrelid != 0 &&
3015			bms_is_member(sjinfo->ojrelid, forced_null_var->varnullingrels))
3016			return true;
3017	}
3018
3019	return false;
3020}
3021
3022/*
3023 * add_base_clause_to_rel
3024 *		Add 'restrictinfo' as a baserestrictinfo to the base relation denoted
3025 *		by 'relid'.  We offer some simple prechecks to try to determine if the
3026 *		qual is always true, in which case we ignore it rather than add it.
3027 *		If we detect the qual is always false, we replace it with
3028 *		constant-FALSE.
3029 */
3030static void
3031add_base_clause_to_rel(PlannerInfo *root, Index relid,
3032					   RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3033{
3034	RelOptInfo *rel = find_base_rel(root, relid);
3035	RangeTblEntry *rte = root->simple_rte_array[relid];
3036
3037	Assert(bms_membership(restrictinfo->required_relids) == BMS_SINGLETON);
3038
3039	/*
3040	 * For inheritance parent tables, we must always record the RestrictInfo
3041	 * in baserestrictinfo as is.  If we were to transform or skip adding it,
3042	 * then the original wouldn't be available in apply_child_basequals. Since
3043	 * there are two RangeTblEntries for inheritance parents, one with
3044	 * inh==true and the other with inh==false, we're still able to apply this
3045	 * optimization to the inh==false one.  The inh==true one is what
3046	 * apply_child_basequals() sees, whereas the inh==false one is what's used
3047	 * for the scan node in the final plan.
3048	 *
3049	 * We make an exception to this for partitioned tables.  For these, we
3050	 * always apply the constant-TRUE and constant-FALSE transformations.  A
3051	 * qual which is either of these for a partitioned table must also be that
3052	 * for all of its child partitions.
3053	 */
3054	if (!rte->inh || rte->relkind == RELKIND_PARTITIONED_TABLE)
3055	{
3056		/* Don't add the clause if it is always true */
3057		if (restriction_is_always_true(root, restrictinfo))
3058			return;
3059
3060		/*
3061		 * Substitute the origin qual with constant-FALSE if it is provably
3062		 * always false.
3063		 *
3064		 * Note that we need to keep the same rinfo_serial, since it is in
3065		 * practice the same condition.  We also need to reset the
3066		 * last_rinfo_serial counter, which is essential to ensure that the
3067		 * RestrictInfos for the "same" qual condition get identical serial
3068		 * numbers (see deconstruct_distribute_oj_quals).
3069		 */
3070		if (restriction_is_always_false(root, restrictinfo))
3071		{
3072			int			save_rinfo_serial = restrictinfo->rinfo_serial;
3073			int			save_last_rinfo_serial = root->last_rinfo_serial;
3074
3075			restrictinfo = make_restrictinfo(root,
3076											 (Expr *) makeBoolConst(false, false),
3077											 restrictinfo->is_pushed_down,
3078											 restrictinfo->has_clone,
3079											 restrictinfo->is_clone,
3080											 restrictinfo->pseudoconstant,
3081											 0, /* security_level */
3082											 restrictinfo->required_relids,
3083											 restrictinfo->incompatible_relids,
3084											 restrictinfo->outer_relids);
3085			restrictinfo->rinfo_serial = save_rinfo_serial;
3086			root->last_rinfo_serial = save_last_rinfo_serial;
3087		}
3088	}
3089
3090	/* Add clause to rel's restriction list */
3091	rel->baserestrictinfo = lappend(rel->baserestrictinfo, restrictinfo);
3092
3093	/* Update security level info */
3094	rel->baserestrict_min_security = Min(rel->baserestrict_min_security,
3095										 restrictinfo->security_level);
3096}
3097
3098/*
3099 * expr_is_nonnullable
3100 *	  Check to see if the Expr cannot be NULL
3101 *
3102 * If the Expr is a simple Var that is defined NOT NULL and meanwhile is not
3103 * nulled by any outer joins, then we can know that it cannot be NULL.
3104 */
3105static bool
3106expr_is_nonnullable(PlannerInfo *root, Expr *expr)
3107{
3108	RelOptInfo *rel;
3109	Var		   *var;
3110
3111	/* For now only check simple Vars */
3112	if (!IsA(expr, Var))
3113		return false;
3114
3115	var = (Var *) expr;
3116
3117	/* could the Var be nulled by any outer joins? */
3118	if (!bms_is_empty(var->varnullingrels))
3119		return false;
3120
3121	/* system columns cannot be NULL */
3122	if (var->varattno < 0)
3123		return true;
3124
3125	/* is the column defined NOT NULL? */
3126	rel = find_base_rel(root, var->varno);
3127	if (var->varattno > 0 &&
3128		bms_is_member(var->varattno, rel->notnullattnums))
3129		return true;
3130
3131	return false;
3132}
3133
3134/*
3135 * restriction_is_always_true
3136 *	  Check to see if the RestrictInfo is always true.
3137 *
3138 * Currently we only check for NullTest quals and OR clauses that include
3139 * NullTest quals.  We may extend it in the future.
3140 */
3141bool
3142restriction_is_always_true(PlannerInfo *root,
3143						   RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3144{
3145	/*
3146	 * For a clone clause, we don't have a reliable way to determine if the
3147	 * input expression of a NullTest is non-nullable: nullingrel bits in
3148	 * clone clauses may not reflect reality, so we dare not draw conclusions
3149	 * from clones about whether Vars are guaranteed not-null.
3150	 */
3151	if (restrictinfo->has_clone || restrictinfo->is_clone)
3152		return false;
3153
3154	/* Check for NullTest qual */
3155	if (IsA(restrictinfo->clause, NullTest))
3156	{
3157		NullTest   *nulltest = (NullTest *) restrictinfo->clause;
3158
3159		/* is this NullTest an IS_NOT_NULL qual? */
3160		if (nulltest->nulltesttype != IS_NOT_NULL)
3161			return false;
3162
3163		/*
3164		 * Empty rows can appear NULL in some contexts and NOT NULL in others,
3165		 * so avoid this optimization for row expressions.
3166		 */
3167		if (nulltest->argisrow)
3168			return false;
3169
3170		return expr_is_nonnullable(root, nulltest->arg);
3171	}
3172
3173	/* If it's an OR, check its sub-clauses */
3174	if (restriction_is_or_clause(restrictinfo))
3175	{
3176		ListCell   *lc;
3177
3178		Assert(is_orclause(restrictinfo->orclause));
3179
3180		/*
3181		 * if any of the given OR branches is provably always true then the
3182		 * entire condition is true.
3183		 */
3184		foreach(lc, ((BoolExpr *) restrictinfo->orclause)->args)
3185		{
3186			Node	   *orarg = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
3187
3188			if (!IsA(orarg, RestrictInfo))
3189				continue;
3190
3191			if (restriction_is_always_true(root, (RestrictInfo *) orarg))
3192				return true;
3193		}
3194	}
3195
3196	return false;
3197}
3198
3199/*
3200 * restriction_is_always_false
3201 *	  Check to see if the RestrictInfo is always false.
3202 *
3203 * Currently we only check for NullTest quals and OR clauses that include
3204 * NullTest quals.  We may extend it in the future.
3205 */
3206bool
3207restriction_is_always_false(PlannerInfo *root,
3208							RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3209{
3210	/*
3211	 * For a clone clause, we don't have a reliable way to determine if the
3212	 * input expression of a NullTest is non-nullable: nullingrel bits in
3213	 * clone clauses may not reflect reality, so we dare not draw conclusions
3214	 * from clones about whether Vars are guaranteed not-null.
3215	 */
3216	if (restrictinfo->has_clone || restrictinfo->is_clone)
3217		return false;
3218
3219	/* Check for NullTest qual */
3220	if (IsA(restrictinfo->clause, NullTest))
3221	{
3222		NullTest   *nulltest = (NullTest *) restrictinfo->clause;
3223
3224		/* is this NullTest an IS_NULL qual? */
3225		if (nulltest->nulltesttype != IS_NULL)
3226			return false;
3227
3228		/*
3229		 * Empty rows can appear NULL in some contexts and NOT NULL in others,
3230		 * so avoid this optimization for row expressions.
3231		 */
3232		if (nulltest->argisrow)
3233			return false;
3234
3235		return expr_is_nonnullable(root, nulltest->arg);
3236	}
3237
3238	/* If it's an OR, check its sub-clauses */
3239	if (restriction_is_or_clause(restrictinfo))
3240	{
3241		ListCell   *lc;
3242
3243		Assert(is_orclause(restrictinfo->orclause));
3244
3245		/*
3246		 * Currently, when processing OR expressions, we only return true when
3247		 * all of the OR branches are always false.  This could perhaps be
3248		 * expanded to remove OR branches that are provably false.  This may
3249		 * be a useful thing to do as it could result in the OR being left
3250		 * with a single arg.  That's useful as it would allow the OR
3251		 * condition to be replaced with its single argument which may allow
3252		 * use of an index for faster filtering on the remaining condition.
3253		 */
3254		foreach(lc, ((BoolExpr *) restrictinfo->orclause)->args)
3255		{
3256			Node	   *orarg = (Node *) lfirst(lc);
3257
3258			if (!IsA(orarg, RestrictInfo) ||
3259				!restriction_is_always_false(root, (RestrictInfo *) orarg))
3260				return false;
3261		}
3262		return true;
3263	}
3264
3265	return false;
3266}
3267
3268/*
3269 * distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels
3270 *	  Push a completed RestrictInfo into the proper restriction or join
3271 *	  clause list(s).
3272 *
3273 * This is the last step of distribute_qual_to_rels() for ordinary qual
3274 * clauses.  Clauses that are interesting for equivalence-class processing
3275 * are diverted to the EC machinery, but may ultimately get fed back here.
3276 */
3277void
3278distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels(PlannerInfo *root,
3279								RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3280{
3281	Relids		relids = restrictinfo->required_relids;
3282
3283	if (!bms_is_empty(relids))
3284	{
3285		int			relid;
3286
3287		if (bms_get_singleton_member(relids, &relid))
3288		{
3289			/*
3290			 * There is only one relation participating in the clause, so it
3291			 * is a restriction clause for that relation.
3292			 */
3293			add_base_clause_to_rel(root, relid, restrictinfo);
3294		}
3295		else
3296		{
3297			/*
3298			 * The clause is a join clause, since there is more than one rel
3299			 * in its relid set.
3300			 */
3301
3302			/*
3303			 * Check for hashjoinable operators.  (We don't bother setting the
3304			 * hashjoin info except in true join clauses.)
3305			 */
3306			check_hashjoinable(restrictinfo);
3307
3308			/*
3309			 * Likewise, check if the clause is suitable to be used with a
3310			 * Memoize node to cache inner tuples during a parameterized
3311			 * nested loop.
3312			 */
3313			check_memoizable(restrictinfo);
3314
3315			/*
3316			 * Add clause to the join lists of all the relevant relations.
3317			 */
3318			add_join_clause_to_rels(root, restrictinfo, relids);
3319		}
3320	}
3321	else
3322	{
3323		/*
3324		 * clause references no rels, and therefore we have no place to attach
3325		 * it.  Shouldn't get here if callers are working properly.
3326		 */
3327		elog(ERROR, "cannot cope with variable-free clause");
3328	}
3329}
3330
3331/*
3332 * process_implied_equality
3333 *	  Create a restrictinfo item that says "item1 op item2", and push it
3334 *	  into the appropriate lists.  (In practice opno is always a btree
3335 *	  equality operator.)
3336 *
3337 * "qualscope" is the nominal syntactic level to impute to the restrictinfo.
3338 * This must contain at least all the rels used in the expressions, but it
3339 * is used only to set the qual application level when both exprs are
3340 * variable-free.  (Hence, it should usually match the join domain in which
3341 * the clause applies.)  Otherwise the qual is applied at the lowest join
3342 * level that provides all its variables.
3343 *
3344 * "security_level" is the security level to assign to the new restrictinfo.
3345 *
3346 * "both_const" indicates whether both items are known pseudo-constant;
3347 * in this case it is worth applying eval_const_expressions() in case we
3348 * can produce constant TRUE or constant FALSE.  (Otherwise it's not,
3349 * because the expressions went through eval_const_expressions already.)
3350 *
3351 * Returns the generated RestrictInfo, if any.  The result will be NULL
3352 * if both_const is true and we successfully reduced the clause to
3353 * constant TRUE.
3354 *
3355 * Note: this function will copy item1 and item2, but it is caller's
3356 * responsibility to make sure that the Relids parameters are fresh copies
3357 * not shared with other uses.
3358 *
3359 * Note: we do not do initialize_mergeclause_eclasses() here.  It is
3360 * caller's responsibility that left_ec/right_ec be set as necessary.
3361 */
3362RestrictInfo *
3363process_implied_equality(PlannerInfo *root,
3364						 Oid opno,
3365						 Oid collation,
3366						 Expr *item1,
3367						 Expr *item2,
3368						 Relids qualscope,
3369						 Index security_level,
3370						 bool both_const)
3371{
3372	RestrictInfo *restrictinfo;
3373	Node	   *clause;
3374	Relids		relids;
3375	bool		pseudoconstant = false;
3376
3377	/*
3378	 * Build the new clause.  Copy to ensure it shares no substructure with
3379	 * original (this is necessary in case there are subselects in there...)
3380	 */
3381	clause = (Node *) make_opclause(opno,
3382									BOOLOID,	/* opresulttype */
3383									false,	/* opretset */
3384									copyObject(item1),
3385									copyObject(item2),
3386									InvalidOid,
3387									collation);
3388
3389	/* If both constant, try to reduce to a boolean constant. */
3390	if (both_const)
3391	{
3392		clause = eval_const_expressions(root, clause);
3393
3394		/* If we produced const TRUE, just drop the clause */
3395		if (clause && IsA(clause, Const))
3396		{
3397			Const	   *cclause = (Const *) clause;
3398
3399			Assert(cclause->consttype == BOOLOID);
3400			if (!cclause->constisnull && DatumGetBool(cclause->constvalue))
3401				return NULL;
3402		}
3403	}
3404
3405	/*
3406	 * The rest of this is a very cut-down version of distribute_qual_to_rels.
3407	 * We can skip most of the work therein, but there are a couple of special
3408	 * cases we still have to handle.
3409	 *
3410	 * Retrieve all relids mentioned within the possibly-simplified clause.
3411	 */
3412	relids = pull_varnos(root, clause);
3413	Assert(bms_is_subset(relids, qualscope));
3414
3415	/*
3416	 * If the clause is variable-free, our normal heuristic for pushing it
3417	 * down to just the mentioned rels doesn't work, because there are none.
3418	 * Apply it as a gating qual at the appropriate level (see comments for
3419	 * get_join_domain_min_rels).
3420	 */
3421	if (bms_is_empty(relids))
3422	{
3423		/* eval at join domain's safe level */
3424		relids = get_join_domain_min_rels(root, qualscope);
3425		/* mark as gating qual */
3426		pseudoconstant = true;
3427		/* tell createplan.c to check for gating quals */
3428		root->hasPseudoConstantQuals = true;
3429	}
3430
3431	/*
3432	 * Build the RestrictInfo node itself.
3433	 */
3434	restrictinfo = make_restrictinfo(root,
3435									 (Expr *) clause,
3436									 true,	/* is_pushed_down */
3437									 false, /* !has_clone */
3438									 false, /* !is_clone */
3439									 pseudoconstant,
3440									 security_level,
3441									 relids,
3442									 NULL,	/* incompatible_relids */
3443									 NULL); /* outer_relids */
3444
3445	/*
3446	 * If it's a join clause, add vars used in the clause to targetlists of
3447	 * their relations, so that they will be emitted by the plan nodes that
3448	 * scan those relations (else they won't be available at the join node!).
3449	 *
3450	 * Typically, we'd have already done this when the component expressions
3451	 * were first seen by distribute_qual_to_rels; but it is possible that
3452	 * some of the Vars could have missed having that done because they only
3453	 * appeared in single-relation clauses originally.  So do it here for
3454	 * safety.
3455	 *
3456	 * See also rebuild_joinclause_attr_needed, which has to partially repeat
3457	 * this work after removal of an outer join.  (Since we will put this
3458	 * clause into the joininfo lists, that function needn't do any extra work
3459	 * to find it.)
3460	 */
3461	if (bms_membership(relids) == BMS_MULTIPLE)
3462	{
3463		List	   *vars = pull_var_clause(clause,
3464										   PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
3465										   PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
3466										   PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
3467
3468		add_vars_to_targetlist(root, vars, relids);
3469		list_free(vars);
3470	}
3471
3472	/*
3473	 * Check mergejoinability.  This will usually succeed, since the op came
3474	 * from an EquivalenceClass; but we could have reduced the original clause
3475	 * to a constant.
3476	 */
3477	check_mergejoinable(restrictinfo);
3478
3479	/*
3480	 * Note we don't do initialize_mergeclause_eclasses(); the caller can
3481	 * handle that much more cheaply than we can.  It's okay to call
3482	 * distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels() before that happens.
3483	 */
3484
3485	/*
3486	 * Push the new clause into all the appropriate restrictinfo lists.
3487	 */
3488	distribute_restrictinfo_to_rels(root, restrictinfo);
3489
3490	return restrictinfo;
3491}
3492
3493/*
3494 * build_implied_join_equality --- build a RestrictInfo for a derived equality
3495 *
3496 * This overlaps the functionality of process_implied_equality(), but we
3497 * must not push the RestrictInfo into the joininfo tree.
3498 *
3499 * Note: this function will copy item1 and item2, but it is caller's
3500 * responsibility to make sure that the Relids parameters are fresh copies
3501 * not shared with other uses.
3502 *
3503 * Note: we do not do initialize_mergeclause_eclasses() here.  It is
3504 * caller's responsibility that left_ec/right_ec be set as necessary.
3505 */
3506RestrictInfo *
3507build_implied_join_equality(PlannerInfo *root,
3508							Oid opno,
3509							Oid collation,
3510							Expr *item1,
3511							Expr *item2,
3512							Relids qualscope,
3513							Index security_level)
3514{
3515	RestrictInfo *restrictinfo;
3516	Expr	   *clause;
3517
3518	/*
3519	 * Build the new clause.  Copy to ensure it shares no substructure with
3520	 * original (this is necessary in case there are subselects in there...)
3521	 */
3522	clause = make_opclause(opno,
3523						   BOOLOID, /* opresulttype */
3524						   false,	/* opretset */
3525						   copyObject(item1),
3526						   copyObject(item2),
3527						   InvalidOid,
3528						   collation);
3529
3530	/*
3531	 * Build the RestrictInfo node itself.
3532	 */
3533	restrictinfo = make_restrictinfo(root,
3534									 clause,
3535									 true,	/* is_pushed_down */
3536									 false, /* !has_clone */
3537									 false, /* !is_clone */
3538									 false, /* pseudoconstant */
3539									 security_level,	/* security_level */
3540									 qualscope, /* required_relids */
3541									 NULL,	/* incompatible_relids */
3542									 NULL); /* outer_relids */
3543
3544	/* Set mergejoinability/hashjoinability flags */
3545	check_mergejoinable(restrictinfo);
3546	check_hashjoinable(restrictinfo);
3547	check_memoizable(restrictinfo);
3548
3549	return restrictinfo;
3550}
3551
3552/*
3553 * get_join_domain_min_rels
3554 *	  Identify the appropriate join level for derived quals belonging
3555 *	  to the join domain with the given relids.
3556 *
3557 * When we derive a pseudoconstant (Var-free) clause from an EquivalenceClass,
3558 * we'd ideally apply the clause at the top level of the EC's join domain.
3559 * However, if there are any outer joins inside that domain that get commuted
3560 * with joins outside it, that leads to not finding a correct place to apply
3561 * the clause.  Instead, remove any lower outer joins from the relid set,
3562 * and apply the clause to just the remaining rels.  This still results in a
3563 * correct answer, since if the clause produces FALSE then the LHS of these
3564 * joins will be empty leading to an empty join result.
3565 *
3566 * However, there's no need to remove outer joins if this is the top-level
3567 * join domain of the query, since then there's nothing else to commute with.
3568 *
3569 * Note: it's tempting to use this in distribute_qual_to_rels where it's
3570 * dealing with pseudoconstant quals; but we can't because the necessary
3571 * SpecialJoinInfos aren't all formed at that point.
3572 *
3573 * The result is always freshly palloc'd; we do not modify domain_relids.
3574 */
3575static Relids
3576get_join_domain_min_rels(PlannerInfo *root, Relids domain_relids)
3577{
3578	Relids		result = bms_copy(domain_relids);
3579	ListCell   *lc;
3580
3581	/* Top-level join domain? */
3582	if (bms_equal(result, root->all_query_rels))
3583		return result;
3584
3585	/* Nope, look for lower outer joins that could potentially commute out */
3586	foreach(lc, root->join_info_list)
3587	{
3588		SpecialJoinInfo *sjinfo = (SpecialJoinInfo *) lfirst(lc);
3589
3590		if (sjinfo->jointype == JOIN_LEFT &&
3591			bms_is_member(sjinfo->ojrelid, result))
3592		{
3593			result = bms_del_member(result, sjinfo->ojrelid);
3594			result = bms_del_members(result, sjinfo->syn_righthand);
3595		}
3596	}
3597	return result;
3598}
3599
3600
3601/*
3602 * rebuild_joinclause_attr_needed
3603 *	  Put back attr_needed bits for Vars/PHVs needed for join clauses.
3604 *
3605 * This is used to rebuild attr_needed/ph_needed sets after removal of a
3606 * useless outer join.  It should match what distribute_qual_to_rels did,
3607 * except that we call add_vars_to_attr_needed not add_vars_to_targetlist.
3608 */
3609void
3610rebuild_joinclause_attr_needed(PlannerInfo *root)
3611{
3612	/*
3613	 * We must examine all join clauses, but there's no value in processing
3614	 * any join clause more than once.  So it's slightly annoying that we have
3615	 * to find them via the per-base-relation joininfo lists.  Avoid duplicate
3616	 * processing by tracking the rinfo_serial numbers of join clauses we've
3617	 * already seen.  (This doesn't work for is_clone clauses, so we must
3618	 * waste effort on them.)
3619	 */
3620	Bitmapset  *seen_serials = NULL;
3621	Index		rti;
3622
3623	/* Scan all baserels for join clauses */
3624	for (rti = 1; rti < root->simple_rel_array_size; rti++)
3625	{
3626		RelOptInfo *brel = root->simple_rel_array[rti];
3627		ListCell   *lc;
3628
3629		if (brel == NULL)
3630			continue;
3631		if (brel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
3632			continue;
3633
3634		foreach(lc, brel->joininfo)
3635		{
3636			RestrictInfo *rinfo = (RestrictInfo *) lfirst(lc);
3637			Relids		relids = rinfo->required_relids;
3638
3639			if (!rinfo->is_clone)	/* else serial number is not unique */
3640			{
3641				if (bms_is_member(rinfo->rinfo_serial, seen_serials))
3642					continue;	/* saw it already */
3643				seen_serials = bms_add_member(seen_serials,
3644											  rinfo->rinfo_serial);
3645			}
3646
3647			if (bms_membership(relids) == BMS_MULTIPLE)
3648			{
3649				List	   *vars = pull_var_clause((Node *) rinfo->clause,
3650												   PVC_RECURSE_AGGREGATES |
3651												   PVC_RECURSE_WINDOWFUNCS |
3652												   PVC_INCLUDE_PLACEHOLDERS);
3653				Relids		where_needed;
3654
3655				if (rinfo->is_clone)
3656					where_needed = bms_intersect(relids, root->all_baserels);
3657				else
3658					where_needed = relids;
3659				add_vars_to_attr_needed(root, vars, where_needed);
3660				list_free(vars);
3661			}
3662		}
3663	}
3664}
3665
3666
3667/*
3668 * match_foreign_keys_to_quals
3669 *		Match foreign-key constraints to equivalence classes and join quals
3670 *
3671 * The idea here is to see which query join conditions match equality
3672 * constraints of a foreign-key relationship.  For such join conditions,
3673 * we can use the FK semantics to make selectivity estimates that are more
3674 * reliable than estimating from statistics, especially for multiple-column
3675 * FKs, where the normal assumption of independent conditions tends to fail.
3676 *
3677 * In this function we annotate the ForeignKeyOptInfos in root->fkey_list
3678 * with info about which eclasses and join qual clauses they match, and
3679 * discard any ForeignKeyOptInfos that are irrelevant for the query.
3680 */
3681void
3682match_foreign_keys_to_quals(PlannerInfo *root)
3683{
3684	List	   *newlist = NIL;
3685	ListCell   *lc;
3686
3687	foreach(lc, root->fkey_list)
3688	{
3689		ForeignKeyOptInfo *fkinfo = (ForeignKeyOptInfo *) lfirst(lc);
3690		RelOptInfo *con_rel;
3691		RelOptInfo *ref_rel;
3692		int			colno;
3693
3694		/*
3695		 * Either relid might identify a rel that is in the query's rtable but
3696		 * isn't referenced by the jointree, or has been removed by join
3697		 * removal, so that it won't have a RelOptInfo.  Hence don't use
3698		 * find_base_rel() here.  We can ignore such FKs.
3699		 */
3700		if (fkinfo->con_relid >= root->simple_rel_array_size ||
3701			fkinfo->ref_relid >= root->simple_rel_array_size)
3702			continue;			/* just paranoia */
3703		con_rel = root->simple_rel_array[fkinfo->con_relid];
3704		if (con_rel == NULL)
3705			continue;
3706		ref_rel = root->simple_rel_array[fkinfo->ref_relid];
3707		if (ref_rel == NULL)
3708			continue;
3709
3710		/*
3711		 * Ignore FK unless both rels are baserels.  This gets rid of FKs that
3712		 * link to inheritance child rels (otherrels).
3713		 */
3714		if (con_rel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL ||
3715			ref_rel->reloptkind != RELOPT_BASEREL)
3716			continue;
3717
3718		/*
3719		 * Scan the columns and try to match them to eclasses and quals.
3720		 *
3721		 * Note: for simple inner joins, any match should be in an eclass.
3722		 * "Loose" quals that syntactically match an FK equality must have
3723		 * been rejected for EC status because they are outer-join quals or
3724		 * similar.  We can still consider them to match the FK.
3725		 */
3726		for (colno = 0; colno < fkinfo->nkeys; colno++)
3727		{
3728			EquivalenceClass *ec;
3729			AttrNumber	con_attno,
3730						ref_attno;
3731			Oid			fpeqop;
3732			ListCell   *lc2;
3733
3734			ec = match_eclasses_to_foreign_key_col(root, fkinfo, colno);
3735			/* Don't bother looking for loose quals if we got an EC match */
3736			if (ec != NULL)
3737			{
3738				fkinfo->nmatched_ec++;
3739				if (ec->ec_has_const)
3740					fkinfo->nconst_ec++;
3741				continue;
3742			}
3743
3744			/*
3745			 * Scan joininfo list for relevant clauses.  Either rel's joininfo
3746			 * list would do equally well; we use con_rel's.
3747			 */
3748			con_attno = fkinfo->conkey[colno];
3749			ref_attno = fkinfo->confkey[colno];
3750			fpeqop = InvalidOid;	/* we'll look this up only if needed */
3751
3752			foreach(lc2, con_rel->joininfo)
3753			{
3754				RestrictInfo *rinfo = (RestrictInfo *) lfirst(lc2);
3755				OpExpr	   *clause = (OpExpr *) rinfo->clause;
3756				Var		   *leftvar;
3757				Var		   *rightvar;
3758
3759				/* Only binary OpExprs are useful for consideration */
3760				if (!IsA(clause, OpExpr) ||
3761					list_length(clause->args) != 2)
3762					continue;
3763				leftvar = (Var *) get_leftop((Expr *) clause);
3764				rightvar = (Var *) get_rightop((Expr *) clause);
3765
3766				/* Operands must be Vars, possibly with RelabelType */
3767				while (leftvar && IsA(leftvar, RelabelType))
3768					leftvar = (Var *) ((RelabelType *) leftvar)->arg;
3769				if (!(leftvar && IsA(leftvar, Var)))
3770					continue;
3771				while (rightvar && IsA(rightvar, RelabelType))
3772					rightvar = (Var *) ((RelabelType *) rightvar)->arg;
3773				if (!(rightvar && IsA(rightvar, Var)))
3774					continue;
3775
3776				/* Now try to match the vars to the current foreign key cols */
3777				if (fkinfo->ref_relid == leftvar->varno &&
3778					ref_attno == leftvar->varattno &&
3779					fkinfo->con_relid == rightvar->varno &&
3780					con_attno == rightvar->varattno)
3781				{
3782					/* Vars match, but is it the right operator? */
3783					if (clause->opno == fkinfo->conpfeqop[colno])
3784					{
3785						fkinfo->rinfos[colno] = lappend(fkinfo->rinfos[colno],
3786														rinfo);
3787						fkinfo->nmatched_ri++;
3788					}
3789				}
3790				else if (fkinfo->ref_relid == rightvar->varno &&
3791						 ref_attno == rightvar->varattno &&
3792						 fkinfo->con_relid == leftvar->varno &&
3793						 con_attno == leftvar->varattno)
3794				{
3795					/*
3796					 * Reverse match, must check commutator operator.  Look it
3797					 * up if we didn't already.  (In the worst case we might
3798					 * do multiple lookups here, but that would require an FK
3799					 * equality operator without commutator, which is
3800					 * unlikely.)
3801					 */
3802					if (!OidIsValid(fpeqop))
3803						fpeqop = get_commutator(fkinfo->conpfeqop[colno]);
3804					if (clause->opno == fpeqop)
3805					{
3806						fkinfo->rinfos[colno] = lappend(fkinfo->rinfos[colno],
3807														rinfo);
3808						fkinfo->nmatched_ri++;
3809					}
3810				}
3811			}
3812			/* If we found any matching loose quals, count col as matched */
3813			if (fkinfo->rinfos[colno])
3814				fkinfo->nmatched_rcols++;
3815		}
3816
3817		/*
3818		 * Currently, we drop multicolumn FKs that aren't fully matched to the
3819		 * query.  Later we might figure out how to derive some sort of
3820		 * estimate from them, in which case this test should be weakened to
3821		 * "if ((fkinfo->nmatched_ec + fkinfo->nmatched_rcols) > 0)".
3822		 */
3823		if ((fkinfo->nmatched_ec + fkinfo->nmatched_rcols) == fkinfo->nkeys)
3824			newlist = lappend(newlist, fkinfo);
3825	}
3826	/* Replace fkey_list, thereby discarding any useless entries */
3827	root->fkey_list = newlist;
3828}
3829
3830
3831/*****************************************************************************
3832 *
3833 *	 CHECKS FOR MERGEJOINABLE AND HASHJOINABLE CLAUSES
3834 *
3835 *****************************************************************************/
3836
3837/*
3838 * check_mergejoinable
3839 *	  If the restrictinfo's clause is mergejoinable, set the mergejoin
3840 *	  info fields in the restrictinfo.
3841 *
3842 *	  Currently, we support mergejoin for binary opclauses where
3843 *	  the operator is a mergejoinable operator.  The arguments can be
3844 *	  anything --- as long as there are no volatile functions in them.
3845 */
3846static void
3847check_mergejoinable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3848{
3849	Expr	   *clause = restrictinfo->clause;
3850	Oid			opno;
3851	Node	   *leftarg;
3852
3853	if (restrictinfo->pseudoconstant)
3854		return;
3855	if (!is_opclause(clause))
3856		return;
3857	if (list_length(((OpExpr *) clause)->args) != 2)
3858		return;
3859
3860	opno = ((OpExpr *) clause)->opno;
3861	leftarg = linitial(((OpExpr *) clause)->args);
3862
3863	if (op_mergejoinable(opno, exprType(leftarg)) &&
3864		!contain_volatile_functions((Node *) restrictinfo))
3865		restrictinfo->mergeopfamilies = get_mergejoin_opfamilies(opno);
3866
3867	/*
3868	 * Note: op_mergejoinable is just a hint; if we fail to find the operator
3869	 * in any btree opfamilies, mergeopfamilies remains NIL and so the clause
3870	 * is not treated as mergejoinable.
3871	 */
3872}
3873
3874/*
3875 * check_hashjoinable
3876 *	  If the restrictinfo's clause is hashjoinable, set the hashjoin
3877 *	  info fields in the restrictinfo.
3878 *
3879 *	  Currently, we support hashjoin for binary opclauses where
3880 *	  the operator is a hashjoinable operator.  The arguments can be
3881 *	  anything --- as long as there are no volatile functions in them.
3882 */
3883static void
3884check_hashjoinable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3885{
3886	Expr	   *clause = restrictinfo->clause;
3887	Oid			opno;
3888	Node	   *leftarg;
3889
3890	if (restrictinfo->pseudoconstant)
3891		return;
3892	if (!is_opclause(clause))
3893		return;
3894	if (list_length(((OpExpr *) clause)->args) != 2)
3895		return;
3896
3897	opno = ((OpExpr *) clause)->opno;
3898	leftarg = linitial(((OpExpr *) clause)->args);
3899
3900	if (op_hashjoinable(opno, exprType(leftarg)) &&
3901		!contain_volatile_functions((Node *) restrictinfo))
3902		restrictinfo->hashjoinoperator = opno;
3903}
3904
3905/*
3906 * check_memoizable
3907 *	  If the restrictinfo's clause is suitable to be used for a Memoize node,
3908 *	  set the left_hasheqoperator and right_hasheqoperator to the hash equality
3909 *	  operator that will be needed during caching.
3910 */
3911static void
3912check_memoizable(RestrictInfo *restrictinfo)
3913{
3914	TypeCacheEntry *typentry;
3915	Expr	   *clause = restrictinfo->clause;
3916	Oid			lefttype;
3917	Oid			righttype;
3918
3919	if (restrictinfo->pseudoconstant)
3920		return;
3921	if (!is_opclause(clause))
3922		return;
3923	if (list_length(((OpExpr *) clause)->args) != 2)
3924		return;
3925
3926	lefttype = exprType(linitial(((OpExpr *) clause)->args));
3927
3928	typentry = lookup_type_cache(lefttype, TYPECACHE_HASH_PROC |
3929								 TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR);
3930
3931	if (OidIsValid(typentry->hash_proc) && OidIsValid(typentry->eq_opr))
3932		restrictinfo->left_hasheqoperator = typentry->eq_opr;
3933
3934	righttype = exprType(lsecond(((OpExpr *) clause)->args));
3935
3936	/*
3937	 * Lookup the right type, unless it's the same as the left type, in which
3938	 * case typentry is already pointing to the required TypeCacheEntry.
3939	 */
3940	if (lefttype != righttype)
3941		typentry = lookup_type_cache(righttype, TYPECACHE_HASH_PROC |
3942									 TYPECACHE_EQ_OPR);
3943
3944	if (OidIsValid(typentry->hash_proc) && OidIsValid(typentry->eq_opr))
3945		restrictinfo->right_hasheqoperator = typentry->eq_opr;
3946}
